label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: How do you win the Lottery?
Answer: How to win state and national lotteries.
Do you want to win a BIG, BIG prize? Do you want to have all your financial worries sorted out? Do you want to win the lottery? The secret is very, very simple. Anybody can manage it. All you have to do is buy a ticket with the numbers that are going to be drawn at the next draw. If you do this I can GUARANTEE you will win the lottery.

 That's not exactly new information, is it?

Neither is the idea that nobody can predict what those numbers will be. However, when a lottery machine selects the numbers this is in no sense a random action. It is 100% predictable. There is nothing stopping anybody calculating exactly which balls not only will, but MUST be drawn. Nothing stopping you. It will simply take a measuring and timing tolerance at the edge of possibility combined with a huge series of calculations.

You can measure the balls and know their properties. You can time the intervals between starting and stopping the contra-rotating tumble dryers. You can project the trajectories of every single ball and their interactions with each other. It can be done. But it would probably cost more than the size of the lottery jackpot to fund the project and nobody is going to be very happy with people standing around and timing the balls to that degree of accuracy, because one microsecond will totally disrupt all the calculations. The lottery ball machine is totally predictable and not in any way random, but exactly the same thing can be said about a woman. Both are determined and predictable systems, but they simulate randomness far more elegantly than true randomness would. True randomness is a willo-the-wisp entity, like infinity, the highest prime number and the function of half of the roundabouts in Warrington. Lottery machines are not random, but they are chaotic, and therefore, like the minds of women, very difficult to probe with the crude analytical tools we have at our disposal.

So, I can't tell you how to win the lottery. However, I can offer you some interesting advice that you might find helpful.

You have to be in it to win it
This is a very common slogan used by lotteries and football pools companies alike. What this slogan is saying is you can't dream of winning the lottery or the pools unless you part with your beer tokens. Of course they are right. If you never buy a ticket you can never win. But how many times do you dream of winning? Do you have to win every week to be happy? Of course not. You only have to win once. So you only have to buy one ticket, at some time in the future to be able to legitimately dream of winning a huge jackpot.

One ticket will do
Your chance of winning a share in a big jackpot prize is very low. In the UK national lottery (now named Lotto) your chances of having a share of a top jackpot, by getting 6 balls out of 6 representing the numbers 1 to 49 are 1 in 13,983,816. 

Now most weeks people win, often several people share the jackpot. So it does happen. But if you staked £5 every week on those odds you would not have a realistic expectation of winning a share in a jackpot in a normal lifetime. But you knew that anyway, didn't you?

So you might as well get used to the idea that winning the lottery is unlikely. But it's also nice to have a dream, it is good for your mental health. So here is the secret: Buy a ticket. One day. Maybe tomorrow. Maybe not.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: How to calculate circuit breaker rating?
Answer: Add the loads in amperes of each of the appliances on that circuit.  Make sure that the total is less than the ampere rating of the circuit breaker.  You should probably only use about 3/4 of the amperes the circuit breaker is rated for.  For example, a 20 Amp breaker should only be loaded with about 15 Amps.\n\nIf only the wattage of an appliance is given, divide the wattage by the voltage to obtain the amperes.  For example, a toaster may draw 1200 watts.  1200 watts / 120 V = 10 Amps.  A light bulb may draw 60 watts.  60 watts / 120 V = .5 Amps.

sentence:
Title: What in the world is a "Chitlin"?
Question: I was driving through Georgia on Christmas eve and saw a restaurant sign for the nastiest-sounding foods...\nFried chitlin? what the heck is that, anyways?
Answer: Chitlin's, a contraction of the word "chitterlings" are pig intestines, and the people of Salley fry them by the ton during the celebration which is held on Saturday following Thanksgiving.\n\nThe South is famous for its ability to take what most would consider non-useable portions of the animal and turn them into regional delicacies. Pig's feet, hog jowls, ham hocks, cracklin's, and chitlin's are all part of Southern tradition.

label: Family & Relationships
sentence:
Title: Is it wrong to pick up a girl on the rebound.?
Question: This girl just got dissed by a guy she really liked, so is it wrong to pick her up on the rebound.... She knows I have the biggest crush on her.....
Answer: Wrong?  That's for you to decide, but here's some food for thought.

She needs your friendship.  If you give her friendship without going for anything more right now, it shows her respect.  Then, when she is less vulnerable, if the two of you get together she will respect you more and you will be able to trust that she's with you for the right reasons and not simply out of need.

If the two of you got involved right now, it might be for the wrong reasons at the wrong time and thus set you up for failure down the road.

If you are "right" together, then it won't hurt for you to be her best friend and hold off on the other stuff for a while out of respect for her temporary vulnerability and genuine love or care for her.

Just something to consider.  Good luck!

label: Health
sentence:
Title: Advice urgently needed about withdrawal from triclyclic antidepressant,, extreme neck pain ?
Question: I have taken lofepramine (UK name) a tricyclic antidepressant fro around 1 year.  I followed my doctors advice regarding tapering off the dose.  I am fine emotionally and mentally, however the extrem neck pain is really upsetting and disrupting my life (I have two young kids, though my bloke does help !).  I know upper neck and jaw pain is a common w/d symptom, I can cope if it is just short term but have tried every legal possible painkiller and it doenst change. this is third day (and night). I can't sleep for the pain.  Can anyone advise. ?
Answer: I can only advise from my own experience.  Please tell your doctor that you think you need a muscle relaxant for a little while until this neck pain goes away.  Just ask to take one at bedtime because they might make you drowsy.  

Since this is neck and jaw pain, you are probably also a person who grits their teeth at night.  You need to relax.  If you don't mind my suggestions, there are different types of non prescription medications such a Benadryl that you can take at night to help you relax, but I would check with your doctor for a muscle relaxant.  

I am so sorry you are having this problem.  It sounds more to me like anxiety than anything else.  Either that, or you may be developing arthritis in your neck in which case you also need something to relax.    

BE sure and check with your doctor, this could be more than withdrawal.  I suffered with this until it got so bad, I could not turn my neck at all.  I kept taking Ibuprofen constantly and it did not help at all. Just made me feel funny in the head.  Finally, I found I had arthritis in my neck as well as some spurs that had formed on my neck that were pressing on my nerves.  I was given some medication that helped out a lot and I don't know your names in the UK but your doctor can help you with this.  Do you have chiropractic medicine where you live?  If so, you would benefit greatly from this as well as getting a massage from a licensed masseuse.

Best wishes.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: why snow falls in simla and kashmir, why not in ooty?
Answer: Ooty is situated at a lower altitude than simla & kashmir.

Moreover both Simla & Kasmhir are situated either between the ranges or at the foot of the great Himalayas. So the moist wind gets obstracted there, which causes a snowfall in high altitudes & rainfall in lower altitudes. The north west monsoon mainly causes the snowfall as it comes from cold Middle east.The obstraction of this NEM causes snowfall in Kashmir & Simla.

Western Ghats are not as high as the Himalayas. Moreover when the NEM arrives in Western Ghats, they have already lost the last traces of moisture that they contained. 

That is why snowfall does not occur in Ooty.

The Snake is a phallic symbol, the legs emphasizing this.

A less sexually charged interpretation is that:
The Snake with legs symbolizes deception, it biting your child most likeley symbolizes that you fear your child will be hurt. The strange roads represent a period of uneasiness in your life, and the Parents represent the authority and guidance to help you along.

Question: What is RAM (radar absorbent material) made of?
Answer: Most often, especially for low speed applications, it is a matrix of carbon fibers in a foam substrate.  At higher speeds, carbon fiber composites can be used, but radar waves can even be reflected from insulating materials (due to the change in dielectric constant between air and the material).  Many of these things reflect very poorly, though.  Coating the front of your car would not likely help with the local police radar -- however.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: Everything i think of is operant conditioning, in terms of parenting.I cant for the life of me think of any cl
Answer: The importance of empathy for moral socialisation was one of the reason for the development of the violence inhibition mechanism model of psychopathy.34 43 44 This model was also prompted by work suggesting that most social animals possess mechanisms for the control of aggression.45 46 They noted that submission cues displayed to a conspecific aggressor terminate attacksfor example, an aggressor dog will cease fighting if its opponent bares its throat. The violence inhibition mechanism (VIM) is considered to be a functionally similar mechanism in humans where sad facial affects (distress cues) function as a human submission response. At its simplest, the VIM is thought to be a system that when activated by distress cues, the sad and fearful expressions of others, results in increased autonomic activity, attention and activation of the brain stem threat response system (usually resulting in freezing).43 According to the model, moral socialisation occurs through the pairing of the activation of the mechanism by distress cues with representations of the acts which caused the distress cues (moral transgressionsfor example, one person hitting another).43 A process of classical conditioning results in these representations of moral transgressions becoming triggers for the mechanism. The appropriately developing child thus initially finds the pain of others' aversive and then, through socialisation, thoughts of acts that cause pain to others aversive also. \n\nBlair has proposed that psychopathic persons have had disruption to this system such that representations of acts that cause harm to others do not become triggers for the VIM.43 This is thought to occur because the signal to the learning system concerning emotionally aversive stimuli is mutedthat is, the unconditioned stimulus (US) signal is diminished, thus impairing the formation of US-conditioned stimulus (CS) associations. Certainly, the autonomic response of psychopathic persons to the aversive US of sad and fearful facial expressions is muted relative to controls.34 At the neural level, this has been attributed to dysfunction within the amygdala.23 47

label: Health
sentence:
Question: What causes a yeast infection and how do you stop it from itching so much??
Answer: Basically, a yeast infection is caused when there is a build up of yeast in the system. This happens a lot to people who have an excess of Candida Albicans in the system (caused by eating too much refined sugar and white flour and not enough whole foods, fruits and vegetables), and can also happen from a buildup of bacteria locally. If you wear the same underwear for too long, or wear a wet bathing suit for longer than half a day, it can happen quite easily. Some women get minor Yeast infections every month right before their period, and the period usually resolves the infection. They're normal, you just have to make sure you see a doctor if you get them TOO regularly, because this could mean you have an auntoimmune disorder you're unaware of. Also, you want to go to a doctor the first time you think you have one so he or she can make sure it's not something else; a lot of Sexually Transmited Infections can mimic Yeast Infections, as can Vaginitis and Bacterial Infections. It's best to check with your doctor, but if you already have and are looking for a good anti-itch remedy, put some plain yogurt on the itchy areas and the 'good' pro-biotic bacteria in the yogurt will fight the 'bad' bacteria, helping to soothe the itch and reinforce the good bacteria.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: How does a retired educator get started in the business of being an auditor for government grants?
Answer: An excellent resource you need to read is "A GUIDEBOOK FOR FEDERAL GRANT REVIEWERS" by Karen A. Morison http://www.heritage.org/Research/GovernmentReform/FedBook.cfm

The Guidebook states that the qualifications desired in grant reviewers are:

- Evaluate and apply criteria related to program requirements. 
- Read and analyze applications (e.g., for strengths and weaknesses). 
- Write clearly, accurately, concisely, and effectively. 
- Communicate effectively. 
- Contribute to an effective group process by being cooperative, constructive, and flexible. 
- Maintain strict confidentiality. 

Τhe pay ranges from $100-$300 per day, plus travel and expenses, for a total of $500-$1,500. Sometimes you get paid on Friday when you turn in all your signed forms; sometimes your pay is mailed to you or deposited directly into your bank account up to about six weeks later. You will have to pay taxes for the amounts considered income. 

To apply, you need to contact the federal agency who runs the grant programs you are interested in reviewing.

Question: What does white privilege mean to you?  Do you think it exists in western society?
Answer: White privilege means the attitude of a certain group of people in the Caucasian race that go through life without recognizing that they have an air of entitlement about them as they move through life.  A perfect example:  My son and I were at a mall,  he opened the door for me and two Caucasian women, talking to each other, pushed through in front of me, without acknowledging him.  He looked at me and just shook his head.  Another time, a man walked up as I was dealing with a customer service agent in a Cingular store and proceeded to tell the agent his issue.  He became very rude when I explained that I was not only there conducting business as he walked up, but that he needed to get in line behind the other 15 people that he had stepped in front of.  He proceeded to tell me how out of line I was and that he was 'just' asking the agent one or two questions.  I explained to him a little more forcefully that he was not the only one with questions, and needed to wait his turn.  He was so out done he actually left the store.  I've experienced this type of behavior over 50 years in various states, so these are not isolated incidents.\n\nThis is not about slavery or being "owed a living by the government".  This is about being treated as a piece of the woodwork, about people talking about you while you are in the room as though you are not in the room saying things that are offensive and ignorant.  This is about people treating you as though you are invisible which you are to them, stepping in front of you in line, demanding to be served first in a restaurant because their time is more important than yours, walking into you and not saying excuse me but saying excuse me to the white woman behind you that they did the same thing to.  Yes, it is rude, but it is also an attitude of I am better than you and I deserve what I get because I was born to it and I don't give it a second thought.  It's about bringing up slavery, when no one was even mentioning  or thinking of slavery.

label: Family & Relationships
sentence:
Question: are engagement parties just another money grab?
Answer: Here's the thing, most people don't know what an engagement party really is. An engagement party is supposed to be the affair when your engagement is announced to your friends and family (except for your immediate family who should already know). Most people have started using an engagement party as an excuse for gifts since everyone invited already knows, which is just tacky. You don't have an engagement party if everyone already know your engaged. Traditionally the bride's parents host an engagement party. The bride's mother sends out invites to a dinner party or barbeque or luncheon, whatever type of party it's going to be. The bride's father announces the engagement at some point during the beginning of the party.

Question: What is a Novation Confirmation ?
Answer: “Novation” means: (i) the Parties successfully arrange or provide for the substitution of the credit of the specified DCO, directly or indirectly, for the credit of the Parties with respect to Cleared Transactions; and (ii) the Parties’ rights and obligations pursuant to any Transaction (or portion thereof) which becomes a Cleared Transaction are settled, cancelled, extinguished and discharged simultaneously with the DCO’s acceptance of such Transaction (or portion thereof) for clearing; or (iii) any procedures of the specified DCO having the effect of (i) and (ii) above.\n“Novation Confirmation” means a confirmation provided by Party A to Party B substantially in the form set out in SCHEDULE B to the Agreement.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: How can I buy on ebay from Albania?
Answer: If I'm understanding your question correctly, you want to know what items you can order from eBay that will ship to Albania...

Go to eBay's home page, and click on the "Advanced Search" link in the upper right corner of the page. Put in the keywords and categories for the item(s) you are looking for, but DON'T click "Search" or hit "Enter". Scroll down to the "Location" section, and click to select the "Items available to" option. Click the drop-down menu, and select "Albania" as the country. Go through the page and select anything else you want to use to narrow your search (price range, currency, etc.) and click on the "Search" button at the bottom of the page.  In theory, this should limit your searches to sellers willing to ship the items you were looking for to Albania.

Hope this helps, good luck finding what you're looking for! :)

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Question: is the majority leader the same as speaker of the house of representives cause i heard steny hoyer is the lead
Answer: NO.
The Speaker of the United States House of Representatives is the presiding officer of the lower house of Congress, the House of Representatives. The current Speaker is Dennis Hastert, a Republican Congressman from Illinois, whose term will expire at the end of the 109th Congress on January 3, 2007. On November 16, Nancy Pelosi, the House Democratic leader, was nominated by her party to be Speaker of the House for the 110th Congress; she will then be the first woman to hold the office.  The office of Speaker was created by the Constitution of the United States. The Speaker is elected by the House of Representatives, and is its highest-ranking officer.  In the Presidential line of succession, the Speaker is second in the line, after the Vice President of the United States.  

The Majority Leader of the United States House of Representatives acts as the leader of the party that has a majority control of the seats in the house (currently at least 218 of the 435 seats). The Majority Leader works with the Speaker of the House and the Majority Whip to coordinate ideas and supporting votes for legislation.   Rep. Steny Hoyer was selected to be the next majority leader for the 110 Congress on November 16.  His term will start on January 3, 2007 as well.  Rep. John Boehner from Ohio is the current majority leader in the House.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: sales people often think you must be passionate about what you sell, but this is wrong, isn't it?
Question: you should be passionate about how you can help your prospect with your product, people buy for their own reasons, not because you are passionate about your product, right ?
Answer: I think I agree with what you're saying but to me it amounts to very similar things. Firstly, being "passionate" about your service/product doesn't necessarily mean that you dream about it night and day and climb onto a couch and bounce around while discussing its merits. It simply means that you convincingly believe that the offering is a valuable one. Realistically you also understand that this value may differ depending upon the needs of your potential clients.\n\nAnd so, a sales person may be passionate about Honda's because they realize that a Honda can be an extremely cost efficient and reliable automobile with strong performance for a consumer that values those attributes. However, if the defining attribute that the consumer is looking for is prestige, the Honda sales person will appreciate that a BMW or Mercedes may be a better fit. (Of course, you're unlikely to find this understanding in their sales pitch.)

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: what is the highest interest rate a person can get with buying a house?
Answer: the highest rate?
I can give you 200% interest if you want ;)

There are "loan sharking" laws in most areas but they do not normally apply to houses and vary from place to place.

When a borrower is charged interest above an established legal rate. Depending on where you live, lenders typically cannot charge more than 60% interest per annum.

Laws against "usury" go back to biblical times and were adopted by states starting in the 18th century. The concept was simple: People in dire need of money will pay just about any interest rate to get it. So society moved to protect them from sleazy lenders who threaten to break legs.

In New York, for instance, it's a crime for an individual to charge more than 25% interest on a loan. But what's illegal for a loan shark in New York is perfectly legal for credit card issuers that put their operations in states that have no rate ceilings, such as South Dakota and Delaware. They can charge their customers everywhere whatever they wish, because what counts is the law of the state where their credit card operations are based.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: Can anyone assist me?
Question: MATH HELP PLEASE............
1.)Find the equation in the y = mx + b form of the line that contains the point (3,2) and has a slope of 4/3.

2.)The slope of the line 3y = x - 3 is
Answer: We can use the point-slope form of the equation and then transform it into the slope-intercept form.

(y - y1) = m(x - x1)
(y - 2) = 4/3(x - 3)
y - 2 = (4/3)x - 4
y  =(4/3)x - 2.

m = 4/3, b = -2

3y = x - 3
y = (1/3)x - 1

The slope m = 1/3

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Question: Is America, and Democratic nation?
Answer: If you're asking if America is a democratic nation, then the answer is yes.

The United States of America is actually a democratic republic.  It is a democracy, in the fact that there is direct election of representatives.  However, these representatives are the ones that actually make the decisions, the ones that make the laws (hence the Republic).  

A republic is a form of government that has a special "class" or elect that make the governmental decisions (laws, etc) that govern the nation.  In our case, the elect are the representatives and senators at the federal level, as well as your local representatives (state, local) that make the decisions.  

Because these members are elected, then we have a democracy as well.  However, a true democracy would require the votes of everyone on every issue (direct democracy).  It would be a little bit tough with a population of 300 million.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Question: What is the full form of ALOHA protocol ?
Answer: ALOHA PROTOCOL:

Aloha, also called the Aloha method, refers to a simple communications scheme in which each source (transmitter) in a network sends data whenever there is a frame to send.  If the frame successfully reaches the destination (receiver), the next frame is sent.  If the frame fails to be received at the destination, it is sent again.  This protocol was originally developed at the University of Hawaii for use with satellite communication systems in the Pacific.

In a wireless broadcast system or a half-duplex two-way link, Aloha works perfectly.  But as networks become more complex, for example in an Ethernet system involving multiple sources and destinations in which data travels many paths at once, trouble occurs because data frames collide (conflict).  The heavier the communications volume, the worse the collision problems become.  The result is degradation of system efficiency, because when two frames collide, the data contained in both frames is lost.

To minimize the number of collisions, thereby optimizing network efficiency and increasing the number of subscribers that can use a given network, a scheme called slotted Aloha was developed.  This system employs signals called beacons that are sent at precise intervals and tell each source when the channel is clear to send a frame.  Further improvement can be realized by a more sophisticated protocol called Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection (CSMA).

In 1070s, Norman Abram son and his colleagues at the University of Hawaii devised a new and elegant method to solve the channel allocation problem. Many researchers have extended their work since then. Although Abranson's work, called the Aloha System, used ground-based radio broadcasting, the basic idea is applicable to any system in which uncoordinated users are completing for the use of a single shared channel.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: What Is financial Internal rate of return?
Answer: The internal rate of return (IRR) is a way to measure the return on an investment. The formal definition can be found here at wikipedia:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_rate_of_return

It is simply a method used to compare different investment scenarios. As different alternatives may involve different sums of money paid at different points in time, it may be difficult to objectively compare them. The IRR simply produces a single number for each alternative, and the one with the highest IRR is chosen as the best one.

What it basically measures is the amount of interest that a bank account would have to pay in order to attain the same cash payments.

Some simple examples:

For instance, if you were to pay $100, and then receive $110 back 1 year later, the IRR is 10%.

If you were to pay $100, then receive $10 back one year later, and then $110 one year after that (two years after the beginning), then the IRR is still 10%.

In general, the IRR is difficult to solve, and must use iterative numerical methods for its solution.

There are also known problems with IRR that make its usefulness somewhat limited.

Let's just say that you had a situation where you received $110 dollars now, and only had to pay $100 one year from now. If you work through the numbers, you would find the IRR to be -10% (note the MINUS).

If instead you received $100 now and had to pay back $100 one year from now, the IRR would be 0%.

Now we can see the problem - clearly the first alternative is favourable, you receive more money UP FRONT than you even need to pay in. Unfortunately, IRR messes up in these kinds of cases and gives you a misleading result.

Question: What is the difference between "guidance" and "regulation"?
Answer: The obvious difference is one compels and the dictates behavior.  When one is guided towards making the right, choice, for instance, the ultimate decision lies with the individual.  When one is regulated to make a particular choice, consequences exist to punish the individual if they happen to make the "wrong" choice.\n\nExample:  Regulation may require individuals to drive no faster than the posted speed limit.  In rainy or snowy conditions, however, most drivers have been instructed (trained, guided) to drive at far slower speeds.

Question: What are some ways that impulses at the synapse can be altered?
Answer: Impulses at the synapse can be altered by:

1.) Up-or-down regulation of receptors

2.) Levels of enzymes that break down excess neurotransmitters (such as MAO, monoamine-oxidase).

3.) Release of Nitric Oxide by post-synaptic neuron

4.) Inhibitory synapse at axon

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: Should coal power continue to be used?
Answer: Well, currently all technologies have their drawback.  I'll go into detail what's up and why:

Nuclear fission - older LWR (light water reactors) are inefficient.  They use less and produce more waste as compared to the newer models using heavy water and sodium cooled reactors.  New generations of nuclear fuel are providing greater efficiencies.  I believe the best would had been the Integral Fast Reactor.  This would had provided proliferation resistant means of producing power.  Waste would had been sorted out with their pyroprocessing methods, and stuff that would be buried would have a half life of no more than 100 years.  It could use up LWR waste and other wastes from lesser efficient breeder reactors to produce power indefinitely.  It could convert at most 20% of waste back to fuel, and reuse.  But with the whole process, the waste leaving would be completely free of actinides and other long lasting radioactive materials.  So, nuclear fission would had been my favorite, if there wasn't such a public outcry for the source.  Until public opinion changes, there will not be any new sources in the US for a while.  Additionally, cost overruns of power plant production (because of a lot of safety mechanisms needed) would deter a lot of investors.

Natural Gas - you know gas.  It's gaining a foothold in the US.  Technologies are becoming more efficient and cleaner.

Nuclear Fusion - Something for the future, but until it's a proven technology, we can't live off of pipe dreams.

Coal - ah, coal.  It is the current favorite source of power in the US.  There are more advanced power plants called coal gasification power plants which uses scrubbers and other means of cleaning up the air.  This is slated to be replaced by natural gas by DOE's estimates.

Wind, Geothermal, and other sources - 
Wind requires expensive equipment that could alter the landscape.  Again, go check on the DOE's website, but they require many windmills to achieve what one powerplant can.  Geothermal requires hot sources in the earth.  Yosemite could be one, if it wasn't preserved.  Look at iceland for that. Solar is expensive and dependant of climate conditions.  It is better in the desert, but in cities with varying climates, we are dependant on a consistant power source.  Tidal sources of energy could be feasible if it didn't alter marine life ecosystems.  Currently it's a newer source of energy.  Scientists haven't perfected the technology to provide a competative means of power source.

My conclusion:
Yes I think coal should be continue to be used for the moment.  Currently, we cannot just change from one source to another because of current technologies and public perception.  There is a great market for this source of power - it won't dissapear in an instant until there are rivalling technologies that could provide comparable rates.  In the end, $$ are the deciding factor between the feasability of one source versus another.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: Why does a dog see fewer colors than people?
Answer: Humans have three types of color receptors (light-sensitive cells) in the retina.

Dogs have only two.  So dogs are not (as some people say) completely color blind.  They can distinguish between blues and yellows ... but not between reds and greens.

The technical terms are that humans are 'trichromatic', and dogs are 'dichromatic.'

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: What is the magnitude of acceleration at the point of maximum extension of a spring?
Question: The period of oscillation of a spring-and-mass system is 0.51s and the amplitude is 4.9cm.  What is the magnitude of the acceleration at the point of maximum extension of the spring?
Answer: The mass distance from the fixed point is A*sin(wt) where A=4.9 cm and w=2*pi/T where period T=0.51 s. Max distance (= max extension of a spring) will be at wt=pi=3.14. Since the acceleration is the second derivative of the distance it is -Aw^2*sin(wt) which at maximum extension of a spring (wt=pi=3.14) is Aw^2 (amplitude) that is 4.9*(2*pi/0.51)^2=743.73 cm/sec^2.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Question: What is a Codec. I'm having trouble playing video files that I'm downloading?
Answer: Codec stands for COder/DECoder.
It encodes video streams into standardized and compressed format while creating a video.
It also decodes video files from various compressed  formats to video streams while playing that video.
Downloaded videos are smaller in size for saving download time, so they are encoded/compressed and need certain kind of codecs to play.
Perhaps your machine doesn't have some of such codecs.
Do this: check the extension of the video you are trying to play.
It might ve .avi or .mp4 or .divx, install DivX codec pack.
Go to google.com and search for "DivX codec setup", download and install it, it's free. restart the machine.
And you can now play those video files.

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Title: What should i do about my son when my husband is a cross dresser?
Question: my son is 7 months old. my husband wants a complete sex change but right now is cross dressing. Im worried. what should i do when my son is old enough to realize this. i wonder what is going to think
Answer: Hmmm?

I don't know that I see the problem.  What's your son going to think when he gets old enough to realize?  He's going to think "Daddy likes to wear girl's clothes.", or, if the sex change has already happened by then, then he'll have another mommy, I'm assuming.  Neither one is going to scar the kid, or even really affect him.  If he grows up knowing that there's nothing wrong or weird with his dad doing that, then he won't think anything of it (so long as the outside world doesn't know and tease him about it).  It'll just be one of the things daddy does, like mommy liking to put on her sweatpants to do the laundry or something similar, you know- just a little idiosyncracy.  And so long as he sees/understands that other men don't necessarily do it, he won't go raiding your closet (unless of course he wants to, which, in that case, you can't change it).  He'll grow up fine and healthy- just approach this with reason and rationality and show your son that you love him and your family is normal.

Good luck!

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: is botulism a food infection or intoxication?
Answer: Botulism is a rare but serious paralytic illness caused by a nerve toxin that is produced by the bacterium Clostridium botulinum. There are three main kinds of botulism. Foodborne botulism is caused by eating foods that contain the botulism toxin. Wound botulism is caused by toxin produced from a wound infected with Clostridium botulinum. Infant botulism is caused by consuming the spores of the botulinum bacteria, which then grow in the intestines and release toxin. All forms of botulism can be fatal and are considered medical emergencies. Foodborne botulism can be especially dangerous because many people can be poisoned by eating a contaminated food.\n\nSo it's an infection caused by a toxin.

label: Health
sentence:
Title: Financial Help With Bariatric Surgery?
Question: Hi.  I need Bariatric Rou en Y.  It is a $24,000 surgery that is not covered by my insurance.  My credit is good but I do not have a home to mortgage or finance.  Does anyone know of a government financial assistance program or anything that may help a middle classer with surgery?
Answer: Everyone else gave you basically the same advice I would.
Your surgeon's office should be able to help you with getting insurance approval. If they can't/won't, think about switching surgeons.  Or insurance companies. 
My husband's companies insurance guy had originally said there was a "rider" which his boss would have to purchase for the whole company that would cover the surgery. We were willing to pay for that rider if it was just a few thousand dollars, and then the offer was withdrawn (probably because they didn't think we'd actually volunteer to pay!).  So find about that option. Even if it's $5,000, that's $19,000 less than the surgery. Look into supplemental insurance that you buy on your own that might pick up some of the costs.
As a last-ditch attempt, you can ask for donations from friends and co-workers.   If you work for a big company, try to get them to donate some money to you by saying how much healthier you will be after your weight loss.  Have a party to raise money--I've done it for other people (not for surgery)--have poker tournaments where the winner gets half the pot and you get the other half.  Find massage therapists and palm readers who will donate half or all their proceeds for treatments during the party.  Combine it with a Body Shop or Tupperware party and ask everyone to round up their payment and give it to you, and have the consultant donate part of the profits to your cause (you must have a friend who sells SOMETHING like this).
You'd be surprised how your friends and loved ones will rally around you if you show that you are determined to raise this money.

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Question: Is capitalism and the concentration of wealth & power in the US undermining democracy?
Answer: Always has, always will.  We must all learn to operate with a more sophisticated understanding of the American use of the term "democracy" in the first instance.  It doesn't mean (and has never meant) that everyone has an equal say in how things get done.  If that were true, we would all have equal access to our elected representatives.  We do not.  Look at the impact of lobbyists, special interests groups, political action committees, and cronyism on our governmental processes.  It's naive to think that we all have a say.  Still, it is important, as individuals, to remain involved in the process and to let our public officials know how THEY can best serve OUR needs.  When enough of us speak in concert, those who listen stay in office; those who don't don't.

Question: What are the symptoms of diabetes?
Answer: Type 2 diabetes almost always has a slow onset (often years), but in type 1, particularly in children, onset may be quite fast (weeks or months). Early symptoms of type 1 diabetes are often polyuria (frequent urination) and polydipsia (increased thirst, and consequent increased fluid intake). There may also be weight loss (despite normal or increased eating), increased appetite, and irreducible fatigue. These symptoms may also manifest in Type 2 diabetes in patients who present with frank poorly controlled diabetes.

Thirst develops because of osmotic effects — sufficiently high glucose (above the 'renal threshold') in the blood is excreted by the kidneys but this requires water to carry it and causes increased fluid loss, which must be replaced. The lost blood volume will be replaced from water held inside body cells, causing dehydration.

Another common presenting symptom is altered vision. Prolonged high blood glucose causes changes in the shape of the lens in the eye, leading to blurred vision and, perhaps, a visit to an optometrist. All unexplained quick changes in eyesight should force a fasting blood glucose test. These are now quick (less than 5 minutes total), inexpensive (materials less than USD $1), and can be safely performed by almost anyone with trivial training.

Especially dangerous symptoms in diabetics include the smell of acetone on the patient's breath (a sign of ketoacidosis), Kussmaul breathing (a rapid, deep breathing), and any altered state of consciousness or arousal (hostility and mania are both possible, as is confusion and lethargy). The most dangerous form of altered consciousness is the so-called "diabetic coma" which produces unconsciousness. Early symptoms of impending diabetic coma include polyuria, nausea, vomiting and abdominal pain, with lethargy and somnolence a later development, progressing to unconsciousness and death if untreated.

sentence:
Title: How do I pick a religion?
Question: I beleive deep down in my heart that there is a God.  The problem is that I was not raised very religiously at all.  As an adult, I've done some research on several different religions.  However, my knowledge of any one religion is very superficial.  Each one seems to make sense on a certain level, yet seem so "out there" on another level.  I don't know if I'll ever be able to fully accept and participate in any religion.  But I feel like I need something to believe in.  I know that there is more to life that what we see.  I want to raise my children to believe in something.  I want some peace in this matter.  Has anyone else had this problem?
Answer: I had this same problem. I was raised Catholic, but very loosely. Religion did not play a major part in my life, but don't think I was ever really an atheist. I started looking into other religions such as different Christian denominations, Hinduism, Bhuddism, Zoroastrianism, Mormonism, Judaism, Sikhism, Shinto, Manicheism, Gnostic Christianity, Wicca and even simply deeper into my original Catholic upbringing. I found them all very interesting, and full of truths, but not complete. Then I looked into Islam and I converted after about a year or so of research  and study of the religion. It just seemed to be what I had already believed all along, for as long as I can remember. \nI'm not saying that you should necesarilly become a Muslim now, but look into it. I would look into other religions on a deeper level and study them to see what makes the most sense and find what you believe (note I say "believe" and "sense" in the same sentence, not "find very interesting"). Until you find something, if you don't come to your own conclusions, just believ in God and do whatyou think is right; there isn't really much room for error there.\nI hope you find what you are looking for.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: How come all the different radio signals don't get all garbled together?
Question: Like Am and FM. Or police, truckers, and EMT signals?
Answer: Different reasons -- 

1.  Frequency separation.  Radio stations operate on different channels (frequencies) than CB radios for example.

2.  Different locations.  You can operate on the same frequencies if the transmitters are located far enough away from each other.  Look how many "Channel 3" TV stations there are.  They're far enough away from each other so they don't interfere.

3.  Different modulation.  FM and AM are types of modulation.  In the case of radio, they're also on different spectrums, but you could have the same frequency modulated differently and have minimal interference.

4.  Digital transmission.  You can transmit information digitally over the airwaves and "multiplex" it such that it doesn't interfere with other transmissions (cell phones for example).

Answer: Pacific Ocean - The deepest known point on Earth is at the bottom of the Mariana Trench, a depression in the floor of the western Pacific Ocean, just east of the Mariana Islands. \n\nThe Mariana Trench is 1,554 miles long and averages 44 miles wide. Within it, about 210 miles southwest of Guam, lies the deepest known point on Earth. Named the “Challenger Deep” for the British survey ship Challenger II that located it in 1951, this underwater gorge plunges to a depth of nearly 7 miles! It is deeper than Mt. Everest is tall.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: what do spdf stand for besides sharp principle diffuse and fundemental?
Question: i heard that they were also named after other words that are from a different language
Answer: s,p,d and f are designations for the four different types of orbitals.

s is spherical in shape and has only one orientation in three dimensional space.

p has a dumbbell shape and has three orientations in 3-d space:
Px,Py and Pz.

d has a double dumbbell shape and has 5 orientations in 3-d space.

f has a complex shape and has 7 orientations.

Each orbital can hold 2 electrons. s having only 1 orientation can hold only 2 electrons. p having 3 orientations, has 3 orbitals, each having the capacity to hold 2 electrons, so it can totally hold (3*2=) 6 electrons.
Similarly d can hold (5*2=) 10 electrons and f can hold (7*2=) 14 electrons.

label: Health
sentence:
Title: what is a Voiding cystourethrogram?
Question: my 15 year old daughter is having one done and i would like to know what happens during the procedure, if she is going to be completely nude, and what she should wear after?
Answer: A VCUG is usually done for recurrent UTIs.  The patient will be asked to undress from the waist down (pants and panties), and be given a gown.  She will be asked to empty her bladder in the bathroom.  Usually, a preliminary x-ray image will be taken.  The only way to see the bladder and uterers (the tubes that connect the bladder to the kidney) with x-ray is to introduce a contrast or dye.  In order to do that, the patient must be catheterized.  The radiologic technologist or possibly a nurse will do this part of the study.  Your daughter will be laying on her back, with her knees bent and spread apart.  The tech/nurse will clean the outside of the genitilia with a betadine soap and insert a small tube through the urethra to the bladder.  This can be very uncomfortable as the tube is passed through the urethra, but once the tube is in place, your daughter will not know it is there.  It is important that she try to relax her muscles as the tube is introduced.  Tight muscles will make the insertion harder and it will take longer, meaning it will prolong the uncomfortable feeling.  Once the tube is in place, the legs are straightened and her gown or even a blanket will be put over her, so that she is not exposed.  At that point, the radiologist will enter the room.  While watching in real time on a TV screen, the radiologist will start allowing the contrast to fill the bladder, via the catheter.  Your daughter will probably be able to see this as well.  Eventually, her bladder will start telling her brain that she needs to urinate.  We want the bladder to get fairly full.  They are looking to make sure the valves at the UPJ (ureteropelvic junction-where the ureters enter the bladder) are competent.  They should not open and allow fluid from the bladder to enter the ureters.  Once your daughter feels full, she will be asked to pee on the x-ray table.  They will put towels between her legs, and she will be covered.  Peeing on the table is important, because sometimes with the pressure, the urine will leave the bladder and enter the ureter.  IF a patient cannot pee on the table (and some cannot for modesty issues), we will take out the catheter tube and have her pee in the toilet in private.  After emptying the bladder, she will be asked to hustle back to the x-ray table for a picture to make sure there is no contrast in the ureters.    There really is no side effects post procedure.  She will not leak contrast or have pain.  So whatever she wants to wear is fine.  The worst part of this study is the embarassment of the catheterization.  And as long as you are not pregnant, you should be allowed to wear a leaded apron and stay in the room with her, if she wishes.\n\nAnother good idea:  When they catheterize your daughter, you can get a "clean catch" of urine.  Make sure they give you some of this clean catch for testing.  When you urinate into a cup, it is possible for contamination from the labia etc.  A clean catch is just urine, and nothing else.  Our pediatric group in town always wants the parent to bring a clean catch to them so they can test it for bacteria.  Find out if this is something they want you to do before the procedure begins.  It may save your daughter from having to be catheterized for a clean catch later on.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: true/false biology question, plz help!...?
Question: Q. In mice, black fur colour is dominant to brown. Answer TRUE/FALSE to the following statements:

(a) Matings of pure breeding black mice with brown mice would yield both black and brown offspring in the ratio 3:1

(b) Matings of pure breeding black mice would yield only black offspring

(c) Matings of brown mice could yield black and brown offspring

(d) Matings of heterozygous black mice with brown mice would yield both black and brown offspring in the ratio of 1:1

and also if possible could you explain how you got your answers! thank you so much, i've racked my brain over and over again with this question and i just can't seem to get it. So explanations are much appreciated as they will help me to understand it a little better. Thank you so much!!
Answer: Let B mean the genotype for dominant black fur and b mean the genotype for brown fur. 

Recall that when a gene is dominant, it is always expressed when  it is present. When a gene is recessive then it is ONLY expressed  when there is no alternative.

a) Pure black mice would have B1B2 and brown mice must be b1b2 because it is a recessive gene. (I've put in the numbers to act as subscripts so it makes more sense.) Crossing these, you would get offspring that inherit one gene from each parent.
B1b1 ==> black
B1b2 ==> black
B2b1 ==> black
B2b2 ==> black

So false because all the offspring from pure black and brown rats would be black. 

b) True. Pure black mice have BB. As both parents have BB then there's not a chance for bb to occur!

c) False. Brown mice are always bb (recessive) so bb x bb can never result in Bb or BB. So offspring of 2 brown mice are always brown.

d) Heterozygous black mice are Bb. So crossed with brown mouse bb (b1b2):
Bb1 ==> black
bb1 ==> brown
Bb2 ==> black
bb2 ==> brown
So you get equal numbers of black and brown offspring. True.

Title: 8xy^3z^2////6x^2y  |||||\/||||  15mn^2p/////25m^2n^2?
Question: With work pleez
Answer: 1) Answer: 4y^2z^2/[3x]
8 and 6 are both divisible by 2.
For the variables, subtract the exponent on bottom from the exponent on top.
(4/3)x^(1-2)y^(3-1)z^2
= (4/3)x^(-1)y^2z^2
Since the exponent on x is negative, it must be moved to the bottom.
= (4/3)y^2z^2/x^1
= 4y^2z^2/[3x]

2) Answer: 3p/[5m]
Similar to the first one.

Title: Is it more correct to average numbers before taking log values, or averaging the log values..cite sources?
Question: I have 3 measurements, which are going to be "logged" (i.e) 3 values... 2,3,4 are to logged... the average of the 3 is log 3 = 0.477, or it correct to go...log 2 = .3, log 3 = .447 and log 4 =.602 and there average is 0.4496.... i know the difference is small, but to me it is important.
Answer: Keep in mind that what you're doing when you add the logs is multiplying the ratios.  Then dividing by 3 takes the cube root of that.  So averaging the values is the arithmetic average and averaging the logs is the geometric average.  Which one is fits what you're doing best?  You'll have to figure that out.  I'd guess the geometric average based on the formula you've given.

label: Health
sentence:
Title: If you have been on Methadone before ...did you have any problem getting off of it  when you tried?
Question: Do you think it is a good solution to an addiction problem or would you suggest an alternative?
Answer: My dad was on methadone for over 30 years. Its one of those controversial treatments. I don't dispute the fact that it is a life saving treatment. There are honest doctors that can use it to wean you off drugs with it and have great success. The large problem areas are the methadone "clinics". Many people go there for treatment expecting a gradual reduction which never comes. Here the cost for methadone is 10-20 per day and either 25 or 50 a month for a counseling session. So they are making too much money off of it. Instead of gradually reducing the amount to get people off of it, they increase the dose as the patients become tolerant of it. There are some private doctors that will do methadone treatment but you really need to discuss it over with several before making a decision. Some of these doctors will try to wean you off of the methadone too fast. You have to find a doctor with a reasonable plan of care. I remember watching HBO a few months ago and there is a special called Methadonia which if you can watch that, they really portray the methadone clinics fairly for the most part. If you do decide to seek treatment at a clinic make sure that your doctor is aware that you are there to quit and not for maintenance therapy! Good luck on trying to help yourself. The type of addiction that requires this type of treatment is a hard journey and congrats on making that decision!

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: math problem, vapor pressures?
Question: Small quantities of oxygen gas are sometimes generated by heating   KClO3   in the presence of   MnO2   as a catalyst:


        2KClO3(s)   →   2KCl(s)   +   3O2(g)


What volume (ml) of   O2   is collected over water at   24.0   oC   by the reaction of   0.275 g   of   KClO3   if the barometric pressure is   727.5 mm Hg  
The vapor pressure of water at   24.0 oC   is   22.4 mm Hg.
Answer: To answer this, you can use the ideal gas law, V = nRT/P

You know R , T  and P, andso you need to find n.

To find n , note that 2 moles of KClO3 produces 3 moles of O2. Given the mass of KClO3, you kind find the moles of this compound by dividing the mass by the molar mass. Assuming that there is 100% conversion, you can use the ration above to find the moles of O2.

Lastly, I'm not sure, but I think you need to use the gage pressure, which is the barometric pressure - the vapor pressure.

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Title: Can anyone help with some useful, daily life type information about Springfield, MO.?
Question: Will be moving there in August if all goes well...would love to know what its like...Thanks
Answer: I live in Springfield and I love it here. I have lived here in the area my whole life and wouldn't trade it for the world. I have traveled all over the country and visited other cities of similar size and I think Springfield is the best. The streets are easy to understand and get around on, the traffic is never that bad. The shopping is pretty decent here and if you want more then Branson is only half an hour away. Or drive to KC, it's only about 2.5 hours away. I don't know if you are familiar with the area but the weather is variable. One day its cold the next its super hot. But its kinda nice that way. We have 4 beautiful seasons and lots of outdoor activities here.  We have several colleges but the population is diverse, its not all kids. Restaurants here are good, lots of choices. Springfield is the birthplace of Cashew Chicken and I think we have like a couple hundred Chinese restaurants. There is one on about every corner.  =)  Springfield is also the home of Brad Pitt and his family if that interests you at all. Though he doesn't visit as much as he used to now that he is with Angelina. =)  If you have any more specific questions or are interested in a certain neighborhood I would be happy to give you my opinion if you send me a message. Hope you get to make the move here to Springfield I think you will like it!

label: Health
sentence:
Title: is it infected????
Question: i got my ears pierced about 2 days ago. i was wondering if my ears were infected because when i rotate my earing when i am cleaning it, it kind of hurts a little. my ears arent swollen ,red, or goo isnt coming out. i was wondering when you rotate your earing when cleaning it and it hurts a little if it is normal
Answer: Yes it was normal for me anyways.  As long as your ear isnt turning red in that spot and staying red and that it isnt oozing good you should be fine. Wash your ears gentle with non scented anti bacterial soap and you should be on your way to healing fast :)

Here is what you should do for taking care of your piercing:

Always wash your hands with soap and water before touching the pierced area or jewelry to keep bacteria from your hands away from the piercing site. Keeping the pierced area clean is the most important way to prevent infection. 

Cleanse the piercing site with appropriate solution as frequently as recommended for that particular site, and for the full length of time recommended for healing. (See third paragraph.)

Keep pierced area free of chemicals such as perfume, hair spray or hair gel. After showering, rinse the pierced area with clear water to remove soap or shampoo residue. 

Do not hold a public telephone against a newly pierced ear. Clean your telephone frequently with a disinfectant. 

Following ear or facial piercing, be sure your pillowcase is clean and changed frequently. 

A gauze pad or cotton swab can be used to clean crusts from around the jewelry. Use a new swab for each piercing site. Jewelry should be rotated once or twice while applying solution during each cleaning. 

Earrings should not be removed or changed for at least a month, and should be worn continually for the first 4 to 6 months. Other piercing sites may require more specific care. 

If you do get an infection here is what you can do:

If signs of infection (increased redness, tenderness, heat, swelling) occur in a piercing that is through cartilage, seek medical treatment with antibiotics immediately. An infection of a piercing in cartilage may form a permanent swollen lump (keloid) on the ear. 

Mild infections of the ear lobe (soft tissue) may be treated with proper cleansing and the local use of antibiotic ointment daily for one to two weeks. Jewelry should be left in to allow drainage of exudate. If no improvement, medical evaluation and treatment is recommended.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: Why do elephants have tusks?
Answer: Elephants have evolved with tusks as the elongated teeth enable the elephants to manipulate and hold items with the trunk and tusks.  They can pick up quite small items and rotate in front of their lips using the trunk and tusk combination to strip off the leaves etc.  They also use the trunk to strip the bark off of trees,scrape earth up to dig water holes and mineral scrapes, and for defence - although their large size makes them fairly predator proof.\nIndian elephants have smaller tusks than African elephants because they have evolved as a jungle species and the bigger tusks would get in the way in the undergrowth etc, plus the lusher vegetation means they do not need to scrape for water etc.

Title: NCLB as a smokescreen for politicians cutting budgets?
Question: Anyone else think NCLB is the government's attempt to cut Education out of the Federal budget, making teachers take the rap so that the politicians don't have to?
Answer: Absolutely. Conservatives have been trying to abolish the Department of Education ever since it was created in the 1970s.

The standards NCLB sets are so ridiculous that most districts will be hard pressed to pass them, and are therefore labeled "failed" schools. If you school fails, your funding is cut off, making it ever more difficult to raise standards because you have ever fewer resources.

It's a well-designed plan to make it look like Bush cares about education, while in reality shafting the public education system.

Title: Having been on holiday to destinations which are known to have a large Catholic following........?
Question: such as Ireland, Spain, Italy and the Phillapines. I have noticed that these countries are also, to my suprise, very tolerent of Gay people. Why is this?
Answer: ok spud, its not just 'gay' people.  it's people in general.

years ago i was an unhappy Baptist.  being baptist just wasn't doing it for me and i had been born and raised in that faith.

a lot of the baptists i knew were mean spirited and hateful. they also had the "we're the only ones going to heaven" attitude.  i began looking for another faith.  that is a HARD thing to do, believe me.

i worked in a pharmacy where one of the employees was obnoxious about his protestant faith.  if you asked him how he was he'd say "He was fine ever since he became a Christian." if you asked him what he knew he'd say "I know I'm saved."  and on and on and on.  there were also a couple of Catholics that worked in the pharmacy.  when steve the protestant was telling people why they were going to hell the Catholics never were mean or hateful to anyone. they accepted everyone, it seemed.  they accepted me---even though i didn't know what i was and they accepted steve even when he was telling them they worshipped statues.

i thought to myself, there has got to be something to what these people believe, i want whatever that is for myself.

i asked them how i could learn more about the Catholic Church and they told me about classes that were given at the local parish.  i went and talked to the priest and he signed me right up.  six months of classes later, i knew i'd found what i was looking for.  i joined the Catholic Church way back in 1980 and its been an important part of my life ever since.

so---i don't think its just gays.  Catholics seem to be tolerant of just about everybody.  And that's a good thing.

label: Health
sentence:
Title: If You eat 800 calories a day will you lose weight?
Question: Hey! I'm really worried about one of my friends she is saying she is fat when she is not. I insist on telling her she is fine the way she is and is beautiful but she thinks she is huge. She's told me she is on this diet that she only eats 800 calories a day. Is this true? is this healthy? will she lose weight? I don't want her to get hurt but she won't listen to me what can I do to help her!!!!!!
Answer: Yes, she will lose weight. And muscle tissue and brain functionality,strength and endurance. She is dangerously below what she needs in a day (usually 1200-1600 calories are recommended for those who are extremely overweight). Sounds like she may need to seek counseling because if she sees something different than what really exists when she looks in the mirror, then her mind's eye needs to be reprogrammed. These are often beginning signs of anorexia and bulimia. Encourage her to talk to a counselor. She is in danger of harming herself by doing these things. This is not the time to keep your thoughts to yourself. If necessary, ask for help from a trusted adult to intervene. She may get mad at you, but it's worth it to save her life. Be encouraged.

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Question: How do i file an appeal?
Answer: The rules regarding appeals will vary from state to state and there are different types of appeals too.  In the most basic form, you will need to file a notice of appeal.  In many states, it is up to the appellant to create the record for the court of appeal by designating what documents the trial court clerk should copy and send to the court to the court fo appeal.  The appellant may also need to pay for and arrange for copies of the trial transcript to be availble for the court of appeal.  AFter the record is complete, you must file an opening brief.  The respondent files an opposition brief and you file a reply.  Sometimes the court will hear oral argument.  This is just the BARE basics, so get an attorney right away.

then go out and give your wife and kids the best day of your life while you still can (assuming you can get out of bed).  

Have a party at the end of the day and invite all of your friends, and go out with a bang.

For yourself, make sure after the party you make love to your wife as many times as you can, and leave her with a smile on her face so she'll never forget you.

Title: I had a mole removed 3 mths ago, leaving a scar.Now a blistery thing has formed over it.Should I be concerned?
Question: It looks like a blister has formed with blood and pus, or maybe a pimple?  It doesn't really hurt.. maybe a litle bit in the shower.  It has been fine for the past 3 months, so I don't know why this happened.  Could it be an infection?
Answer: Its more than likely drainage from the incision under the scar tissue, possibly with a little infection. Have your doctor look at it. S/he will probably just drain it and possibly give you an  antibiotic. Probably nothing to worry about but if its not going away you should have someone check it out. If there is continuing infection under the scar tissue it could go systemic and that would put your vital organs at risk.

Question: explain proportions & ratio?
Answer: ratio is an expression of relative size of two numbers, the numbers could be any things... (ratio of doctors in london to kangaroos in australia)

proportion is the ratio 
1) of a part considered in relation with the whole... (milk in my recipe, doctors in london to population of london), or 
2) statement of equality between two ratios (used only mathematically)

For practice problems on ration proportions, log on to www.mathguru.com and select relevant topics.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Title: Any preschool/toddler  teachers here?
Question: Do you like your job? Would you share your per hour pay? Is 7-10 dollars per hour about the range? Thanks!
Answer: I LOVE my job. It did take a couple of years to get the hang of classroom management. 

When I first started teacher preschool (12 years ago), I made $7 per hour. A lot of schools pay you depending on your education or how many child development units you have. If you have 6-12 units, $10 is not too bad. 

I now have my Masters in Education (60+ units in child development) and make $20 and hour. This is considered good pay in my area (California). Most schools here pay about $12 per hour. The teacher turnover rate is pretty high partially because of this. If you are educated, go for as much as you can get. Enjoy!

Title: ANY one BEEN to KUWAIT or working in KUWAIT or used to workin Kuwait PLEASE ANSWER THIS AND HELP ME PLEASE?
Question: i have a job offer in kuwait, the salary is 1000KD + 350 acomodation + car + annual tickets + medical insurance.

is this a good salary and can i make some savings out of it? if yes how much??

i also need to know are there beach clubs where women can swin without being offended? how much such a membership cost per month?

what is the general cost of living there like grocery shoping for a month? eating out? shoping in general? water, electricity and telephone bills?

are there houses with the kitchen open to the living room? american system? how much would that cost per month and in wich locations can we find it?

please help me i have to give a dicision in less than 10 days and me and my wife will be moving there.

i am 29 married without children yet

i would really appreciate honest answers for all the above 

i appreciate you effort everyone with the informa
Answer: hello ..am kuwaiti..so regarding ur questions:
1. 1000 kd is a good salary unless u wana spoil urself too much ..lol ..i mean with + 350 accomdation ..u can live very nicely plus u do monthly saving ..let me tell u something about livin in kuwait:
1. dinner in a nice resturant for 2 persons = 15 kd 
2. weekly shopping = 40 kd (if u r favor cocking at home)
3. clothes varies similarly to the state ..u can find cheap and u can find brand names
4. gas (small  6 cyl 4-wheel vech.) = 10 kd per week

overall, i guess if u r only 2 persons, then u gona spend on average between 400 - 600 kd per month; that will leave around 400 kd for saving. Just make sure u get a clear idea about the medical inssurance (r they covering it 100%?) ..the only expensive issue her is kids private schools, which u dont ..so ...come spoil urself with a tax free country ..lol ..
now regarding swiming, there is no public places, but i know couple of places private which ur wife can swim freely without any troubles .these places are basically clubs which she should participate in ...i.e. monthly fees ..
regarding american style accomdation, i guess u with that allowance (350 ) u can have very nice sea-view apartment, very nice place called Mahboula, alot of western are living there ..most of western food, especailly fast food is there ...let me know if u need further assistant ..contact me through my messenger or e-mail ..urs ..

label: Family & Relationships
sentence:
Title: My son is getting married. His finance is inviting all of her realitives but he is only allowed to invite 50?
Question: Her family only consists of around 50. Ours consists of around 75-80. He is only allowed to invite 50 in which there will be hard feelings in our family over this if some of them are not invited. He is also paying for part of the weeding with her mother and her.  What is the proper way to handle this?
Answer: I'm planning a wedding and dealing with a mother-in-law... 
(and we're in a similar situation)
If you want more people to come offer to pay for them!  I konw traditionally it's the bride's family that pays for the reception and all that... but if you would really like to have a few more of your family members there then I'd say offer to pay and talk to them about it.  It's a lot to ask for an extra 25-30 people.  That can be thousands.  

Also, Your son is a man.  Let him handle it.  But if you decide to get involved don't do any passive-agressive stuff cause that can get between him and his fiancee.  I'm sure if he had a problem with it he'd speak up.

My future MIL is always blaming me when anything dealing with her son is to her disliking.  They are adults making decisions and plans together.  Speak with them about it and make sure that you view them as doing this together.

And this is just my 'blah blah blahing' but please try to make sure that you're not expecting too much from your son's wedding.  It is his wedding and his fiancee's  wedding.

-To talk about whomever else we're with; especially guys.

Throw me a tampon!" And two seconds later, a little tube slides under the door. I don't think I could do that with a stranger.

Just my theories. I don't really think there's a real explanation.

the buttons (VARS)(1)(1)  to get Y1


For Y3, type  nDeriv(Y2, x, x)

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: Which of the following statements are true for the reaction Ca(OH)2(aq)   +   HCl(aq)   -->?
Question: Which of the following statements are true for the reaction 


Ca(OH)2(aq)   +   HCl(aq)   -->   




a)   Water is a product

b)   When balanced the coefficient of   HCl(aq)   is 2

c)   Only ions appear in the net ionic equation

d)   OH-(aq)   does not appear in the net ionic equation

e)   Cl -(aq)   does not appear in the net ionic equation 



      Examples of species are   (aq)   (s)   (l)   etc.


you must enter all true statements in one lower case group,
in alphabetical order with no spaces 
e.g., if   a)   b)   and   e)   are true you would enter   abe
Answer: a is true
b is true
c is not true
d is true
e is true

Balanced equation is:
Ca(OH)2 + 2HCl ---> CaCl2 + 2H2O

Ionic equation is:
Ca(OH)2(s) + 2H3O(+)(aq) ---> Ca(+2)(aq) + 4H2O(l)

label: Health
sentence:
Title: Whats wrong with me?!?
Question: I just had my heart cruelly broken recently over a simple crush yet I've been devastated. I'm numb and dont feel anything when another guy likes me.. It's already been 3 weeks. Sometimes I just break out crying at home. I've been excercising a lot lately because it makes me feel better.. but the high wears off in a day and im back to being depressed. What's wrong with me?
Answer: Don't believe crying yourself to sleep at nights will help you to overcome a heart break. The first steps are to relax and spend some time with friends and family, not to mention going out and having some fun. These are all to take your mind off "the heart breaker". If that doesn't work, try meeting new people. The aim for all this is to open up your eyes, and allow yourself to see the truth. Which is that you are still a valuable person. Besides, look at it this way, you are not the one losing, he is. They are losing a unique individual.

My personal philosophy in life is that out of everything bad comes something good. Therefore, the bad thing in this situation is losing the person you love, hence ending up with a broken heart. However the good thing that will come out of it is that, with that person out of your life, you will meet someone new. Someone who will accept you and appreciate you for who you are.

So again, I'll ask, can one mend one's broken wings and learn to fly again? Sure you can. Love comes with a lot of risks so before falling, first consider the cost and weigh your feelings.

sentence:
Title: What is the biggest mistake you have made in a real estate transaction?
Question: Buying or selling real estate, what has been your biggest mistake.  Was it the agent you hired, doing rehab work that didn't pay off, not hiring a good inspector, financing? \n\nWhat do you wish you knew then that you know now and will apply to your next real estate transaction?  Thanks!
Answer: The one thing I think about is when I bought a rental property, I made my offer, not knowing about negotiating tactics.  The buyer agent had me sign a dual agency agreement, and I don't think he had my best interests at heart. I offered a low price and ended up paying full price.  I was naive to the fact that he was not on my side, and also, I should have asked for closing costs or some other bargaining chip.  And I should have stood my ground on the slightly lower price, after coming up some.  Being in the investor shoes, the buyer needed me more than I needer her.  I could've walked away, but she needed to sell.  So she probably would've taken the lower price had I stopped.  Overall, I do not regret the investment, it was good, I am also lucky because I didn't order an inspection!!!  (Naive again) Lucky it was in good shape.  But it also could've been a bargaining chip.  Had I gotten a lower price, I'd of had more equity now.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Question: How old is your PC. And whats the typical lifespan.?
Answer: Typically your machine should last anywhere from 3 to 5 years and possibly even beyond. I have a new PC, but I also use my old one and it's from 1999. It's got some quirks but it still runs pretty well. Old age will kill it eventually I know, but for the most part PCs should last a good long time. 

One recommendation is to leave your computer running most of the time. You put more wear and tear on it by starting up and shutting it down than you do by just leaving it running, so if possible- just let it run.

Also after 3 or 4 years you're probably going to NEED a new machine anyhow, simply because there'll be a new version of Windows, new devices your computer can't accomodate, new software that can't run on older machines, etc.

But if you're just wondering how long you can expect your machine to run, I'd say if you take care of it, it could last at least 4 or 5 years and possibly more.

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Question: What was your biggest Thanksgiving disaster - and how could you have prevented it?
Answer: It was the first Thanksgiving after my husband and I started dating.  He drove three hours to spend the holiday weekend with me so I wanted to impress him with a home cooked meal.  I waited until leaving work Wednesday night to go the the grocery store and in the mad push of people, I reached in the bin and grabbed a turkey.  The next afternoon, I placed my turkey in a sink full of water to thaw and began on my side dishes.  After he arrived and we had a drink, he ventured into the kitchen at which point I noticed he was peering down into the sink and frowning.  I asked if something was wrong with the turkey and he said "Yeah . . . it's a duck."  

To my horror, I had picked up a duck and never noticed that this long narrow bird was not a fat round turkey.  I attempted to roast it and while it had a nice flavor, we quickly realized there isn't much meat on a duck.  In addition, my gravy was burnt, my potatoes were salty and the macaroni and cheese was a solid block.  I finally accepted defeat and we ventured out to the local 7-Eleven and had a Thanksgiving hot dog!   

I wouldn't have done a thing to prevent it!!  That night my husband told me he loved me for the first time and we have been together for over twelve years.  He turned out to be an incredible cook and  I have never cooked another Thanksgiving dinner!  And every year we toast our famous Thanksgiving duck!
