Title: math help please (multiple questions)?
Question: hi. i need math help. if you can help me with any of the following i would greatly appricate it.

1. in math what dose subset mean, and how do you determine weather one interger can be a subset of a numer
2. what is a composit number?
3.the graph of a(n) ________ is a portion of a number line
4. the graph of a _______ intervial has one end point
Answer: 1. Set A is subset of set B if and only if any element in A is in B. For example, {2,3,5} is a subset of {1,2,3,4,5}.
 An integer cannot be subset of any set, since itself is not a set.

 2. Composite number is a positive integer, which can be written in product of two positive integers that are bigger than 2. Any positive number other than prime numbers and 1(1 is neither a prime number nor composite number) is composite number.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Title: what is a firewall and what is  a trojan?
Question: please tell me about computer viruses and prevention stratagies.
Answer: (m)

A firewall is a piece of hardware and/or software which functions in a networked environment to prevent some communications forbidden by the security policy, analogous to the function of firewalls in building construction. 

Trojan: Small malicious programs that are delivered secretly or benignly to your computer as executable files (usually through email, chat lines, FTP, Freeware or Shareware programs, MP3s or even movies)-just like the Trojan Horse, innocent looking on the outside, but containing a nasty surprise hidden inside. It is really two programs-one on your computer ("server") and the other on the cracker's ("client"). This server runs automatically every time the computer is started. A Trojan program gives the cracker complete access to your computer. There are thousands of different Trojan programs.  Trojans need user intervention to install (the file needs to be opened) and do not self-replicate like viruses.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: What are the duties of an Administrative Assistant?
Answer: Generally, telephone, document preparation, maintain a database, scheduling appointments, sometimes some desktop publishing, customer service, scheduling meetings, emails, phone calls.... basically what ever is needed to assist and support an executive (male or female).  It depends on the company and the position.  Depending on the industry the company is in and what the department they'll be working in, the duties could be very simple, to very diverse and unusual.

Being and Admin Asst is a great career and will teach you a lot about people.  If you are a people person and like helping people, this is the job for you.  

I've been in the field for 22 years.  I started out as a "secretary" 22 years ago and I worked my way up and became an Office Manager about 15 years ago.  I absolutely love my job.... love my co-workers and wouldn't do anything else.  When I started my job, I knew it was the place for me and I knew it was the job for me.  

It's a terrific field.  Check out this website.  It's a professional organization for Administrative Professionals.  I belong and it has done wonders for me personally and professional.  You won't be sorry.  www.iaap-hq.org   I recommend joining a local chapter.  The friendship and support is absolutely amazing.  To interact and network with others in the same career and position as you is very rewarding.

Good luck!!

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Title: My Dell inspiron 1150 switches off very often by itself. Can anyone help?
Question: I've noticed that this is likely to occur when there's much access to the h-disk, ex: when I'm performing a virus check or when I'm re-coding a film.

Can an over-heating cause the problem?
Any suggestion welcome.
Answer: The main reasons for restarts: 1.bad memory 2.overheating (can either be processor or chipset)   Yours sounds as if it's your processor overheating, though, since it's usually happening when you're using your processor at or near 100% usage.  

Try turning it on, then going into the BIOS, and check your temps there.  If it's idling much above 50°C in the BIOS, then that's almost surely your problem.  More than likely, you just need to go to Wal-Mart, and buy a can of compressed air, and blow all of the dust out of your heatsink.

Question: how can i print my worksheet at excel?
Answer: Before you print, go to File>Print Preview, and look at where the dashed lines fall. These are the extents of the printed pages. Sometimes they can fall in between two columns, making for a very unreadable spreadsheet. You can fix this by changing the column widths or by going to File>Page Set Up and changing from portrait to landscape orientation. When you're happy with where the page extents fall, then go to File>Print.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: Do penguins have fur or feathers, fins or wings?
Answer: Penguins are birds (Aves) in that they have feathers, lay eggs and have forelimbs modified as wings (but in this case used for swimming). They also share all the other characteristics of birds, being warm-blooded (around 40 deg C) and having a reduced reproductive system (one functional ovary or testis).\nLike all other birds, penguins have feathers. Penguin feathers are short, overlapping and densely packed. The outer part of the feather is waterproof while the inner down section traps an insulating layer of air, keeping the penguin warm in the sometimes freezing water. Unlike those of flying birds, the feathers on a penguin wing are very short.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Question: How do i gert rid of the bloodhound virus from my computor?
Answer: How to Clean/Delete the Bloodhound.Exploit.6 virus?

First of all, since this exploit is a real threat be sure your Windows software is up-to-date and patched with all the current critical updates from Microsoft. This will protect your system from other problems as well as this exploit.

1) Open Internet Explorer and type the following in the Address bar

http://windowsupdate.microsoft.com

2) Follow the on screen prompts to download and install any critical updates that are not loaded on your computer.

You can read more about the specific patch for this Microsoft vulnerability by clicking on the link below

http://www.microsoft.com/technet/security/bulletin/ms04-013.mspx

Now that your system is patched against this exploit, you'll want to remove the file that triggered the virus notice. In the majority of cases, these files are stored in the Temporary Internet Files or Temp folder of the computer. Follow these directions to clean out these files.

For Internet Explorer 5 and above, you can follow these directions to clear out temporary files and delete cookies.

1) Open Internet Explorer and click on Tools
2) Click on Internet Options
3) On the General Tab, in the middle of the screen, click on Delete Files
4) You may also want to check the box "Delete all offline content"
5) Click on OK and wait for the hourglass icon to stop after it deletes the temporary internet files
6) You can now click on Delete Cookies and click OK to delete cookies that websites have placed on your hard drive.

To clean up other temporary files on your computer in Windows 98 or higher:

1) Click Start,  Programs (or All Programs), Accessories,  System Tools, Disk Cleanup
2) Choose the correct drive usually C:\
3) Check the boxes in the list and delete the files

You can learn more information about deleting these Temporary Internet Files, cookies, and more by visiting this page

Lastly, run a thorough virus scan of your system or visit Trend Micro Housecall to make sure your system is clean.

Title: Does anyone star wtach?well... read on.......?
Question: I once observed a little 'dot', n it travelled from the pole star allthe way upto the southern horizon , past sirius , touching the zenith...Any explanation...yeah, I AM NOT MAKING STUFF...
Answer: Depending on the speed of this 'objects' transition from Northern sky to southern....

If it was less than 2 hours after sunset, AND if it took 15 or more seconds to make the transition, it was probably a satellite in polar orbit.

If however the transition was RAPID ( 3 seconds or less ), it was probably a meteoroid.

label: Sports
sentence:
Title: How do we play volleyball!?
Question: Can anyone tell me please! I'm goin to join volleyball next skool year and I dont even know how to play!
Answer: First off, if you are joining at the entry level, they will teach you all the basic rules and skills that you need.  But to be a bit more specific, volleyball is a team game which can be played with 2 to 6 members on the court.  A ball is served from behind the service line (the back line of the rectangle on the floor) and the other team receives the ball.  They have three hits to get the ball back to the other side, where that team will have three hits to get the ball back over.   The goal is to hit the ball to the other side, inside the boundaries, and have it hit the floor. This is how you score a point.  \n\nThere are many techniques and hits that you will learn.  It's a lot of fun and good exercise!  Have fun and enjoy!

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: What is the approximate probability that at least 690 of the intervals will contain the unknown µ?
Question: For advertising purposes, the Beef IndustryCouncil needs to estimate the average caloric content µ of 3-ounce top loin steak cuts.  They assume that the caloric content of such steak cuts is normally distributed and σ is known. One thousand independent samples,ak cuts is normally distributed and σ is known. One thousand independent samples,each of size n, are to be drawn and for each sample, the interval ( X – 0.96 (σ/sqrt(n), + X - 1.06(σ/sqrt(n) is constructed. What is the approximate probability that at least 690 of the intervals will contain the unknown µ?
Answer: I assume that you mean that 1.06*se was added to xbar.  I think that's what you mean.  Otherwise your interval was not well defined.  The minimum value is larger than the maximum.

Since you have a normally distributed population with a known sd sigma, then xbar would have a normal distribution.  You subtracted 0.96 standard errors and added 1.06 standard errors, so you would need to look up the area between -0.96 and 1.06 on the standard normal table.

P(xbar - 0.96(sigma/sqrt(n)) < mu < xbar + 1.06(sigma/sqrt(n)))
= P(- 0.96(sigma/sqrt(n)) < mu - xbar < 1.06(sigma/sqrt(n)))
= P(-0.96 < (mu-xbar)/(sigma/sqrt(n)) < 1.06)
= P(-0.96 < z < 1.06)

That would turn out to be 0.8554-0.1685 = 0.6869.  That would be the probablity that mu would be in one of these confidence intervals.

To find the probability that mu will be in at least 690 of the 1000 intervals, you would need to recognize that this is a binomial.  There are n = 1000 trials, each trial has probability of success p = 0.6869, and the trials are independent.  np and n(1-p) are both greater than or equal to 5, so the sample size is large enough.  Using the normal approximation to the binomial, the mean would be 686.9 and the standard deviation would be sqrt(1000*.6869*(1-0.6869)) = 14.67.  Using the continuity correction, I would need to start at a value of 689.5.

z = (689.5-686.9)/14.67 = 0.18

Looking that up on your table, the area to the right is 1-0.5714 = 0.4286.  There is a 42.86% chance of getting at least 690 intervals that contain mu.

Question: theres been a lot of stress in my life,my parents just got a divorce five days before my graduation,i work 70 + hours a week i get these tingling pains down my arm and i get a lot of chest pain its been going on for a few days i dont know what it is ?i get 4 hours or less of sleep everyday and i also notice that i start to bruise easly if i run into something by accident.i try to spend time with my sister and be there for her?i dont know where to start or how to handle most of the stress other than hide it and dont let anybody see it .everybody has always known me to be strong .how can i vent my stress!!!please can somebody give me some advice
Answer: its great that you are strong, but even the strongest can break down if they dont take care of themselves. dont stop taking care of yourself, your health is very important and at least will keep u from getting sick at a time like this. 

first off, take it easy. dont stress about things that you have no control over and cannot be changed. your parents divorce although sad, and hard, will happen whether you stress about it or not. let things take their course, and deal with your feelings, and help those who are weaker deal with theirs. 

keep yourself busy, at school or work, whichever applies. go out with friends, to movies, or the mall, and have some fresh air... take walks with your sister. 

take long hot baths at night... just lay there. 

there must be at least one person out there in the world that you can talk to or trust enough to tell them how you feel.  and just  remember to relax. dont worry about what cant be changed, cause when you wake up tomorrow and the next day, you still wont be able to change them.  

take care of yourself. its the stress that makes you bruise easily. and your chest will stop hurting as well. find an email buddy... its easier to talk to people who dont know you or are far away... at least for me. =)  i have a few good friends around the world.

sentence:
Title: What is degenartive arthritis?
Question: What can I do for it and how I can I get it so I can ride broncos again.
Answer: Degenerative Arthritis is basically joints that are deteriorating.  Joints deteriorate for several reasons.  Previous injury(fractures, ligament tears, repetitive microtrauma), can also be from diet(eating an inflammatory diet of meats, fried foods and carbohydrates) vs. eating plenty of green vegetables and drinking plenty of water to start.  There is a ton of stuff that contribute toward degerative arthritis, the main thing is lifestyle choices that we choose through out our lives.  Lifestyle choices such as exercise, diet, sleep, mental attitude, keeping all joints healthy and mobile.  If you are interested in keeping your joints from getting worse, I would recommend going to a chiropractor.  They are the only ones who will address joint problems.  They will not give you medications which cover up the symptoms of the joint problems, but actually coach you in a way to better joint function and improve your health.  If you want a quick fix, then there is always pain killers or anti-inflammatory medications but that will only help temporarily until your joints completely deteriorate then its time for surgical replacement.  After surgical replacement you chances are very slim that you will ride again, except around the barrels or around the farm.  Hope this information helped you along.  Want more information you can go to our web site www.yourdynamicwellness.net and that will give you plenty.  Key thing, dont just take a drug or surgery unless you completely know the side effects and outcome.\nSincerely,\nDr. Jon Bright

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: What protection does a President of an S Corporation have against Bankruptcy?
Answer: Depends.  Any debts that corporation has only belong to the corporation.  They do not pass through to the president or any other member of any type of corporation, including an S corp.  However, many small business owners foolishly sign joint liability agreements for debts the corporation generates.  In that case, you are liable by contract.  Always read the fine print on any agreements you sign while running your business.  Keep a lawyer on retainer expressly for this purpose.  Consider more aggressive asset protection measures.

Title: Sexual Orientation Questions?
Question: Why does everyone seem to care what someone's sexual orientation is?  Isn't that a personal thing.  Why do "gay" people feel they have to "come out of the closet?", discuss their sex life, etc to the media?  Maybe all the "straight" people should start publicly speaking on their sex life?
Answer: You are so right - a person's sexual orientation should not matter.

As for coming out of the closet - most of the time the gays are simply doing the same thing that straights do all the time: talking about thier family and/or loved ones. When we are talking about media stars - it seems the public has an interest in who is dating/marrying who, etc. Should they only ask those questions to straights? Should gays have to lie when asked these questions?

Stop and think about a typical Monday conversation - talking about what you did over the weekend and usually who you did it with. How it is somehow wrong for a gay man to say that he and his partner went out to dinner and a movie Saturday night but ok for a straight man to say he and his wife did? Do you want the gay man to lie and say he and his "roommate" went so that you will feel more comfortable?  

It's not like being gay is only about sex - like everyone else - sex is only about 10% of a person/relationship. I'd rather focus in on the other 90% of a person.  I just don't get what all the fuss is about - being gay is not contagious.

Question: What does it mean by Bluetooth technology?.?
Answer: Bluetooth technology is a cutting-edge open specification that enables short-range wireless connections between desktop and notebook computers, handhelds, personal digital assistants, mobile phones, camera phones, printers, digital cameras, headsets, keyboards and even a computer mouse. In a nutshell, Bluetooth technology unplugs your digital peripherals and makes cable clutter a thing of the past.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Title: Has anyone experience with attending an online college?
Question: I would like to hear some stories be they good or bad.
Answer: I took a few classes via Onlinelearning.net to clear my teaching credential.  It was the best/easiest thing I could've done.  I was working full time, finishing up my Masters Degree, and trying to keep up with everyday life.

Taking classes online allowed me the opportunity to work when it was convenient for me, which was mostly late at night.  I also didn't have to waste time driving to and from class in traffic.  

Participation with others in the classes took place on discussion boards.  A certain amount of participation was required (i.e. post 5 messages on the message board in one week).  Assignments had to be e-mailed to the profs.  Aside from that, not much was required.  

Overall, it was a great experience.

sentence:
Question: what exactly is herpeys?
Answer: A virus that stays in you forever.  70-80% of the American population have herpes simplex virus A.  Evidenced by cold sores.  B and C are more insidious.  B is genital, transmitted through sexual contact, and C is a blood borne virus that leads to kidney and liver damage.  There is no cure but there is an experimental treatment for simplex C.  It involves a year of weekly injections and can possibly erradicate the virus.  Only C is life threatening.  A and B are transmitted only when the carrier is experiencing a flare up.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: which animal has the longest gestation period??
Answer: Longest Mammal Gestation Period
The Asiatic elephant (Elephas maximus) has an average gestation period of 650 days (more than 21 months), and can be pregnant for up to 760 days – more than two and a half times as long as humans.

source: http://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/index.asp?id=51186 

The longest gestation period known in mammals is that of the African elephant (Loxodonta Africana). These elephants carry their babies in the womb for about 660 days or 22 months. Camels and giraffes carry their babies for about 400 days. Other animals that have gestation periods closest to that of humans are: dolphins - 276 days, chimpanzee - 237 days and cattle - 280 days.

source: http://scienceiq.com/ShowFact.cfm?ID=494

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Title: Do i lack confidence/self-esteem?
Question: I am a really bubbly person around those people i know...but when i meet someone new ...i usualy tend to keep quiet ... usually feel a fear to talk .. which people mistaken for arrogance or attitude problem .. have tried to overcome this problem many times and i feel fustrated that i cant be myself around strangers
Answer: Yes, I'd say you are shy and lack self-confidence.  I know what you mean because I tend to be this way too.  People often think you are unfriendly, when you really are just shy.  At least try to give a shy smile, to let them know you are friendly!  ;)

Something which has helped me is to make a switch in my mind from myself to the other person/people.  Do not focus on yourself but on them.  Have you ever thought about what you think in your own mind when you encounter other people?
Some people think, "What can I get from this person?"  Some think, "What does this other person think of me?"  Or any other various things, but we always are thinking something.  What is helpful is to think, "What can I do for this other person?"  Think "What can I do for you?"

I make this switch quickly and easily by simply saying to myself, "You, not me."

You will be amazed how well this can work, because then you are not self-conscious.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: a planetary nebula exists before what?
Question: before the red giant phases.
before the main sequence.
for a long duration compared to the time the star spent on the main sequence.
or all the above.
Answer: A Planetary Nebula is a misleading name.  At the end of a lower mass star's life (for stars up to about 8 times the mass of the sun), the star puffs up, and loses the outer layers of gas surrounding it.  All this gas goes out into space where it can be seen as what we call a planetary nebula.

Originally the word "nebula" was applied to any astronomical object that was not a star or a memeber of our solar system (planets, asteroids etc), the word essentially means "cloud."

As telescopes got better people started to find many different types of nebulae. A Spiral Nebula looks like, well a spiral, today we know that these are spiral galaxies.  Planetary Nebula were named because the looked round, and a number of them had the same greenish color that Uranus had been seen to have by Hershel.

label: Health
sentence:
Question: What's a quick and HEALTHY way to lose weight?
Answer: Eat right and exercise. Be your own trainer and nutritionist, the basic rules to losing weight are no soda, no fast food, lots of chicken, fish, fruits, vegies, whole wheat stuff, yogurt. Not so many cakes and donuts and cookies. Basic common sense for eating, you just need to enforce it. Get all the bad stuff out of the house right away, just toss it or give it away while your still motivated. No alcohol if that applies. Some juice but make sure its all natural, because the ones that aren't are loaded with sugar and a leading cause in diabetes in children and adults. Lots of water, 3-4 liters a day, not all at once, but spread out. Its really easy to down a liter or two while your watching tv or on the computer. Also drink some after you eat the bigger meals of the day, it helps your body pick out the nutrients in food when your food is more spread apart.  These rules may be hard to adapt to at first so start with cutting out the things you know you can live without.  Then work to there.  Remember these are  perfect eating habits, it can take you a while to work into it.  If you know you can’t live without your one treat a day, don’t fight it by trying to eat something else, just eat it and make sure that’s the only one you cheat on a day.  Otherwise you’ll get depressed and quit.\nAs for exercise, lots of cardio. Find something you like. If you have never exercised much before, start with walking 30 minutes a day at a brisk pace. Move on to different terrains that include hills and increase the time spent. After a week or two, try jogging the route. No matter what find an activity you enjoy. Walking to jogging is just an example, try hiking, rollerblading, biking. Once you get into a regular routine, if your not happy with the level of difficulty, try signing up with a gym. Spinning, pilates or kickboxing classes are great. It will also introduce you to working with weights. Talk to a trainer for instruction before using any weights. A cheap investment is a balance ball, it works you stomach while your just sitting on it, like at your computer and its great for posture. There are hundreds of exercises you can use it for for your entire body. I can give you some ideas if you want, just email me. Also for more info on the benefits of water go to www.watercure.com Good Luck!

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Question: How many people actually know what Kwanzaa is?
Answer: KWANZAA Is the first non-heroic African American holiday ever to come Into existence. Blacks, from every phase of life in the United States, have been practising this holiday since 1965. This warm, social, holiday where people gather to reinforce each others spirit and friendship was founded by Professor Maulana Karenga.
Celebration of this holiday lasts for seven days from December 26 to January 1.
It is a holiday based on the African celebration of the "first fruits harvest" which comes at the end of their year, and KWANZAA lasts seven days to promote the seven basic principles honored during this holiday.

1. Unity - 2. Self determination - 3. Collective work and responsibility 4. Co-operative economics 5. Purpose 6. Creativity & 7. Faith

At KWANZAA gatherings people usually bring fruit or food and share a meal and a smile. The unity cup is passed and people say positive things about Blacks and our future. Then the candles are lit, and something is said about each principle. After that people dance or talk, and just have a good time. There's a lot more to this holiday than superficial gift giving. It's a time to lift each other up and give thanks for being of a unique culture with a value all its own.

They also have a stamp that was created by Synthia St. James who was born in Los Angeles... She is an author of childrens and adult books, artist and has a line of her own clothing

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Title: My roomate has Windows XP 64bit. Is there a way to turn it back to 32 bit so he can install software?
Question: He is having trouble installing software because it is not compatible with the 64bit Windows XP and he does not know what to do.
Answer: Unfortunately, no. The problem is that 32 bit Windows has a subsystem called WOW (Windows on Windows) which emulates a 16 bit Windows environment. Many installation programs use a 16 bit header file which loads the install. 64 bit Windows has WOW32 which loads Win32 programs, but does not have the original (16 bit) WOW to load programs like that.

I think users need to pressure Micro$oft to include WOW with 64 bit versions of Windows. The few good things about Windows has always been backwards compatibility- with 32 bit XP, you can run DOS programs dating back to the original IBM PC- so long as they behave and do not try to access the hardware directly.

EDIT: A user above mentioned partitioning. That is certainly a possibility. Another is to get a program like VMWare or Virtual PC. With that, you can run a 32 bit version of Windows in a virtual PC window and since you are running a 64 bit version of windows with a cpu which supports 32 bit emulation, the performance hit should be minimal. In other words, you run a copy of Windows XP (or 98 or 2000) inside Windows XP 64 bit.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Question: whta is swing in java?
Answer: Swing is a GUI toolkit for Java. Swing is one part of the Java Foundation Classes (JFC). Swing includes graphical user interface (GUI) widgets such as text boxes, buttons, split-panes, and tables.\n\nSwing widgets provide more sophisticated GUI components than the earlier Abstract Windowing Toolkit. Since they are written in pure Java, they run the same on all platforms, unlike the AWT which is tied to the underlying platform's windowing system. Swing supports pluggable look and feel – not by using the native platform's facilities, but by roughly emulating them. This means you can get any supported look and feel on any platform. The disadvantage of lightweight components is possibly slower execution. The advantage is uniform behavior on all platforms.

label: Society & Culture
sentence:
Title: Likes studying Chinese?
Question: Understands China? Has exchanged with the Chinese? Likes studying Chinese?
Answer: I have a few Chinese friends that I have kept for several years.  It is more difficult to have a close friendship with a Chinese person because they are conditioned by their culture to be more formal and guarded.  I lived in China for almost a year.  I still cannot play go very well (weiqi).

I like the way Chinese characters are constructed but it is difficult for me to remember them and they are very small.  I cannot appreciate the beauty of the calligraphy that I would hang it on the wall as a person does a painting.  I know how difficult it is to write with a brush though.  It took longer for me to learn Chinese than to learn French.  It is difficult to get used to the tones.  

I like Chinese foods even the fungi and fermented things.  I miss the sweets.  I like some Chinese art.  I like the children's palaces.  I like the exercising before class and in the park.  I think acupuncture may really work.  Qi gong breathing is healthy.  

China is way too crowded, though.  I really felt claustrophobic.

A. Simply each
1. square root of 10 times cube root of 100

2. square root of 6x times fourth root of 36xsquared

3. cube root of 36xcubey divided by square root of the quantity 2x cube

4. cube root of 54xsquared divided by sixth root of 4xto the forth

5. square root of 8x times square root of 27xyto the fifth all over cube root of 6y

= 10^(1/2) · 10^(2/3)
= 10^(7/6)

label: Health
sentence:
Question: how many people( you know) do you think may have bipolar disorder?
Answer: Actually, bipolar is an unusual disorder that happens to be grossly over diagnosed. Epidemiological studies estimate that that around 1% of the entire population likely meet criteria for bipolar. Yet, psychiatrist in particular seem to like to diagnose this disorder whenever they hear somebody has an elevated mood between bouts of depression or has signs of agitation during a depressive phase. These symptoms may be indicative of bipolar, but bipolar requires much more. As a psychologist at a major medical institution, I can tell you that in the past 5 years I've only seen maybe ten true cases of bipolar, but I've probably seen over 100 patients who were incorrectly diagnosed with this disorder. If you think you have bipolar or a psychiatrist diagnosed you with it, you should get a second opinion from a doctoral level psychologist (i.e., Ph.D. or Psy.D.). The other way bipolar tends to be over used is if somebody you know seems unreliable or overly emotional. Such symptoms do not necessarily indicate bipolar, but the media and untrained physicians will use this phrase almost as a colloquialism.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: who was Dr.Clopton Havers?
Answer: Clopton Havers

1655?-1702

English physician who is known for his study of bone structure and growth, as well as his book Osteologia Nova, which was the first publication to describe bone structure in great detail. The intricate vessel system consisting of canals and glands that supplies nutrients in the bones, called the Haversian system, is named after him. Havers graduated with his medical degree from Cambridge University; he practiced medicine in London and was a Fellow of the Royal Society.

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Title: Tell me more about Dual-Citizenship Mexico-USA and USA-Mexico? Is a Guadalupe Hidalgo Treaty amendment?
Question: Mexicans are a special case on immigration issues, since they were here before anyone else. Comprendes?
Answer: You cannot become a citizen of another country and have dual citizenship in the US.  Dual citizenship can only be a result of being born that way.  For example, if your parents served in the US military in Mexico, as attaches for example, and you were born in Mexico, but your parents submitted your birth certificate to the US embassy as a report of birth abroad, you would be a dual citizen.

The Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo has 23 articles, but I think what you are referring to just outlines that if the Mexican government granted a deed to a tract of land to a Mexican farmer, for example, that the US government; despite annexing that land, would allow the farmer to remain on that land and honor the deed.

It did not extend the right for all Mexicans to enter the US free from control.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: My 79 year old relative wants to buy a second home as a holiday house what are the disadvantages tax wise?
Question: This would be a second home, can she put it into a relatives name (preferably mine!) and if she lives for 7 years it will become outside the inheritance tax bracket?
Answer: Hello

If she buys a second home then gives it to someone else in your family, or she gives someone else in your family the money to buy a home; and she dies within 7 years, then inheritance tax may be payable.

If the total value of her estate, which includes all: property, cars, assets, cash, etc... exceeds £263,000; then 40% tax must be paid on the rest.

If shes gives away a gift (home, cash, etc...) then lives for 7 years then it is safe from inheritace tax.


For example: 
Scenario a)
 - she gives you a £150,000 house;
 - she has another home worth £150,000; 
 - she has £63,000 in funds in the bank; and
 - she dies in 6 years.

Total value of estate: £363,000.
Inheritance tax allowance: £263,000.
Taxable amount after allowance: £100,000.
Total inheritance tax payable: £40,000.

Scenario b)
 - she gives you a £150,000 house;
 - she has another home worth £150,000;  
 - she has £63,000 in funds in the bank; and
 - she dies in 10 years.

Total value of estate: £213,000.
Inheritance tax allowance: £263,000.
Taxable amount after allowance: £0.
Total inheritance tax payable: £0.


Thanks

label: Health
sentence:
Question: How can a person go to sleep in the early evening without drugs.?
Answer: i have always been quite the insomniac. luckily for me, I'm on a night shift now, and seeing as i enjoy staying up late i just do and it's fine. however i wasn't always on a night shift so i can give u some of my techniques.

1.) first of all, u could try herbal supplements. Valerian root and melatonin are the two best. melatonin will always be in pill form that i know of, but it's just a replicate of the exact same chemical that your brain uses to put you to sleep. it's either the best stuff out there or it won't work at all. that depends on you. Valerian root on the other hand, has one major benefit for you. it doesn't to be a pill. u can actually buy this in it's natural form and i think u boil it in some water. the pills for valerian root and melatonin are the most convenient.  there may also be some herbal teas that have it.

2.)ur second bet is herbal teas. there are alot of them available a sleep aid and even if they don't have Valerian root they do have other things that work. and drinking something warm before u go to bed usually helps you go to sleep anyhow. warm milk can sometimes do it for me. you need to make sure, however, that you do not drink anything with sugar or caffeine in it at least 2 hours before you go to bed. if i have to go to bed at ten, i don't drink caffeine after 5 pm, or not at all if i can help it. also, u might try not smoking about a half hour before sleeping if u r a smoker.

3.) another thing u can try is a sleep CD. my friend Whitney swears by some of them. they have music or sounds or just gentle whispering and she says that some of hers will put u to sleep within 5 minutes of starting them.

4.) make sure that you have the most comfortable bed set up possible. if your like me, your body and mind love finding any excuse to get up, and the wrong pillow blanket and mattress is an easy target. i love the foam pillows but the foam mattresses suck for me. but it all depends on you. if you sleep in clothes, make sure they are soft and loose. it's better to sleep in the nude, however. also keeping a room a bit too cold for ur tastes but having plenty of cover is a great way to go to sleep. you'll wrap urself up tightly and u won;t move. soon you'll be asleep. i always sleep better in winter.

5.) reading or watching a television show that just DOES NOT INTEREST YOU is another way. i watch the news because i hate the news. sometimes the news can conk me out in the middle of the day. 

6.)there are plenty of meditation exercises out there and lots of them can relax you to the point of sleepiness. instructions for quite a few should be available on line, but try this one: breath in and fill your lungs all the way up. imagine that when you breathe in you are breathing in a number (the number one for the first breath and so on). fill ur lungs up completely with ones. then breath out all of the ones (emptying ur lungs completely) and then continue with the number two. sounds crazy but it does relax you and if ur laying on ur back in ur bed, it can actually put you to sleep.

i hope one of these works!!

Title: If i took plan B & had an irregular period  6 days later then did it work?
Question: I had a period maybe 2 weeks b4 taking plan b. After taking plan b i got an irregular period 6 days later. Does that mean the pill worked?
Answer: One possible side effect of taking Plan B is that your next period may be altered, it may be early, late, lighter, heavy or it could be the same. Plan B contains hormones which can cause these changes to your next period as well as some spotting/break through bleeding. Any bleeding or changes to your next period after taking Plan B have NO relationship to whether or not the Plan B was effective in preventing pregnancy.

label: Health
sentence:
Question: What is emphysema and what are its causes?
Answer: Q. What is Emphysema?
FAQ

From Stacey Lloyd,
Your Guide to Lung Diseases.

A.

Emphysema is a lung disease in which the walls of the alveoli (air sacs) in the lungs fracture or burst. As these alveoli break, the remaining alveoli become enlarged to make up for the loss. This results in a fewer number of alveoli, with many enlarged. 
When alveoli are enlarged, they loose their elasticity. A loss of elasticity in the alveoli results in the inability to eliminate air from the lungs. Therefore, patients must use force (energy) to expel this air. 

Furthermore, the loss of elasticity in the lungs affects the smaller bronchial tubes. To push air out of the bronchial tubes, a higher pressure must be developed inside the chest. 

This higher pressure tends to collapse the smaller bronchial tubes, making it even more difficult to empty air from the alveoli. 
Coughing becomes very difficult and ineffective in expelling mucus, leaving the lungs more susceptible to lung infections, such as pneumonia. 

( http://lungdiseases.about.com/od/emphysema/f/emphysema_expl.htm )

What causes emphysema?
Emphysema can be caused by one or a combination of these things:

Cigarette smoking: Most cases of emphsyema are caused by cigarette smoking. Cigarette smoke reaches deep into the lungs and causes permanent damage. If you have emphysema from cigarettes, the best way to stop your lung damage is to quit smoking as soon as possible. It's never too late to quit smoking. 
Alpha-1 Antitrypsin deficiency: Some people have emphsyema because of a rare genetic disorder called Alpha-1 Antitrypsin deficiency. People with Alpha-1 are missing an enzyme that protects their lungs. Some people with Alpha-1 deficiency get emphysema without ever having smoked. Other people get emphsyema from the combination of smoking and having Alpha-1 deficiency. 
Air pollution: There is some evidence that air pollution can contribute to people getting emphysema, especially if the person also smokes. ( http://www.lung.ca/diseases-maladies/a-z/emphysema-emphyseme/index_e.php#causes )

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Title: (referring to feudalism in the middle ages) how did serfs ensure that their land would be plentiful?
Question: i need help with my social science homework :-/
Answer: They couldn't. It took many years of improvements in agricultural technology before that could happen. 

The most useful thing to the serfs was the invention of metal plows. Before that they used a wooden scratch plow. This was designed for tilling soil in the Mediterranean region but was horrible for tilling the hard soil further north. When they were able to make deeper furrows they were able to plant their crops deeper which protected them from the elements resulting in a greater crop yield. 

Another important invention was the creation of a new kind of harness that allowed them to work the land with horses instead of oxen. The old design cut off too much of the horse's windpipe. Naturally, a suffocating horse isn't a good worker. So they used oxen which didn't suffocate but were slow and difficult to work with. With the new harness they could use faster horses and work more of the land.

label: Family & Relationships
sentence:
Question: i ve this friend whom im not sure is my friend?she is jealous of me is she a good friend?
Answer: That would depend on the circumstance of what she is jealous of and how she displays her jealousy. A good friend is happy for you and supportive when great things happen in your life. However sometimes even good friends become jealous perhaps because they are going through rough times or they have yet to acieve their own goals or they are lacking love at the moment, any number of reasons. In this instance if you have something they don't and they desire having the same,it is only human nature to be jealous. How the jealousy is displayed determines the depth of character/friendship. Is she laughing and smiling and screeching "I am so jealous, but good for you" or is she tense, a little distant, and crying or scowling. This is unhealthy jealousy, for she is jealous with an envious heart that may create problems for the friendship. If she is vindictive then in her jealousy she may try to bring about the downfall of your happiness. But if she jealous AND supportive, well cut her some slack and try to be empathic.

sentence:
Title: why would i be having strong feelings of love to another man and iam married?
Question: it is really strange, iam married with two kids. at the biggining of our staying together as couples, i was total in love with my husband. i got married when i was still very young at the age of eighteen and at this time round all i could think of was my husband, i loved him very much and i guessed he took advantage of my love to him and could oftently hurt me badly by sleeping outside. he onces addmited tome that he had had an affair,  imagine, with all these i still loved him even though he didnt show any love for me. most of the time i found myself praying to God to change him and make him agood husband to me and of which God did. my husband is now changed and i can feel that he know loves me as never before. unffortunately, now is me who doesn't feel love for him but instead have feelings for another guy who is not married. i have had sex  with this man and enjoyed it so much and since then i dont seem to be getting my mind off him and i dont desire my husband any more! help me!...
Answer: Your situation sounds very similar to my life story.  My wife and I got married very young, and I was not always very nice to her.  I drank a lot, and though I never physically abused her, I did emotionally.
   She ended up becoming distant and looking for emotional relief in other men.  Never physical, but emotional hurts just as much.  I ended up meeting a woman, straying, and falling for her and it almost destroyed my life.
   My wife and I go to counseling, which I suggest for you, and are working extremely hard to repair our marriage.  We are doing a good job, and things in many ways are better than ever, but there is still work for both of us to do.  In the end, ideally, we will have both grown stronger and love each other all the more because of all we have been though with each other.
   This man is probably more of an infatuation than love because you are feeling things that you have missed.  Devote yourself to your husband, and him to you and remake better than before the love you had.
   Get counseling, work it out.  Find you flame again.  If this does not work, than the only option left is to divorce, but this should be your last option, not your only option.

Good luck.

Question: how much retirement money is in 401(k) plans and how is it invested?
Answer: All plans have rules to become eligble first. When can I enroll in the Plan?
You must be 21 years old and have completed at least one year of service with the Company to join the Plan. Then you contribute savings and the company usually has a sliding matching scale  How much can I contribute?
Through automatic payroll deduction, you can contribute between 1% and 25% of your eligible pay on a pretax basis, up to the annual IRS dollar limits.  Does the Company contribute to my account?
The Company helps your retirement savings grow by matching your contributions.
(This is from my plan yours will be different)
"Your company" will contribute $1 for every $1 on the first 3% of pretax pay you contribute, and $.50 for every $1 on the next 2% of pretax pay you contribute. You can access it on-line and change how it is invested  What are my investment options?
To help you meet your investment goals, the Plan offersyou a range of options. You can select a mix of investment options that best suits your goals, time horizon and risk tolerance. The  investment options available through the Plan include conservative, moderately conservative, and moderately aggressive funds. label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: Find the area of a regular hexagon with sides 10?
Answer: Formula of area 'A' of any regular 'n'-sided polygon of each side 'a' is
A = 0.25*n*a^2*cot(pi/n)

Put a=10 and n=6 in this formula.
A = 0.25*6*10^2*cot(pi/6)
  = 150*sqrt(3)   [since cot(pi/6) = sqrt(3)]
  = 259.8 sq units

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: in the case of a divorce can the ex-spouse be named as a dependent if they can't get individual coverage?
Question: My friend is getting divorced in the States but still wants to provide coverage for his ex through his work plan. Can he name her as a dependent?
Answer: An ex-spouse can still retain group medical coverage under COBRA for up to 36 months.  COBRA is a federal mandate for continuing health benefits when a qualifying event occurs such as divorce, for employers with 50 and over full and part time employees.  On the day the divorce becomes final, the employee should notify their employer and paperwork will then be sent to the ex-spouse notifying them of their right to continue medical benefits under the group's plan up to 36 months.  The paperwork must be completed and return within the specified time frame.  The monthly premium payment due is the full amount the insurance carrier charges the employer and the employer can add up to 2% of the cost to administer the paperwork.

label: Sports
sentence:
Question: How many countries participate in the Olympic Games and about how much money is usually spent on the Olympics?
Answer: In last Olympics (2004) 202 countries participated.

However, much to their surprise, hosting the 2004 Olympics has sucked $10-12 billion (in U.S. dollars) out of the Greek economy. That’s more than five percent of the country’s annual gross domestic product! When Greece bid on the games, it was before security was being beefed up around the world as a direct result of the 9/11 terrorist attack. 

Sponsored Links

Top 8 Stocks for 2006

America's 8 Leading Stock Experts Share their Top Picks. Free Report

www.NewsletterAdvisors.com

Mutual Funds

The Latest Online Source You Will Find It Here!
imutualfunds.net

$300 - $1,100 Every Day

Deposited Daily Into Your Bank Start Now, No Experience Necessary

www.WaterhouseReport.com/Online.htm

It’s estimated that $1.5 billion is being spent on security alone. 

Security costs include the posting of 40,000 troops along the northern border prevent illegal entry. The new security system is so sophisticated that it can photograph people’s faces, intercept phone calls and deduce cell phone numbers almost anywhere in the city. 

Television rights, corporate sponsorships and ticket sales help the hosting country bear the burden of the costs, but $12 billion is a lot to come up with. NBC is paying about $800 million for broadcast rights to the Olympics in the U.S. NBC expects to net a record $1 billion in ad sales for its Olympic coverage. Indonesia's major networks decided a $1.35 million price tag was too much, though they are willing to dish out $10 million to air the 2006 FIFA World Cup. 

Ticket sales for the 2004 Olympics have been disappointing, partly due to late construction and partly due to a lack of interest in attending the games among the Greek citizens. Just two weeks before the start of the Olympics, about 3.1 million of 5.3 million tickets remain unsold. 

What remains to be seen is if hosting the 2004 Olympics was worth it. Greek politicians are adamant that late ticket sales and long-term tourist dollars will pay for the costs over time, but many are skeptical. Since the Los Angeles Olympics in 1984, every hosting country has either broken even or made a profit on running the two-week event, but the costs are getting out of control. Breaking even on the Olympics this time around would be a tremendous accomplishment. 

What about the value of the new stadiums? Four years after the Sydney Olympics, under-used venues are costing taxpayers $32 million (in U.S. dollars) a year to keep afloat, and the government expects that some venues will need at least another decade to break even. 

If you think the Greeks overcommitted, the Chinese have budgeted $23 billion for the 2008 Olympic games – seven times more than the Sydney games, and 32 times what Los Angeles spent for the 1984 Games!

Question: What is Finite Element Analysis?
Answer: Finite Element Analysis (FEA) is a process in which you solve equations.  These equations can relate to stress, as mentioned above, but also heat, diffusion, chemical reaction, or anything you want.\n\nFEA is a system of solving these equations in which a large object is broken into many elements.  Then equations are solved for each element and stitched together to give the final answer.  The method is now pretty standard and optimized to give fast reliable results in many cases.

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Title: Microsoft Exchange and Active Directory?
Question: I have View Only Administrator Access in AD(Active Directory), but when I go to Create a user account, the Mail Stores wont show. Any tips?
Answer: A View Only Admin is just what the name suggests--- you're Administrative rights are limited to View.\nIf you need to confirm this, open the properties of one of the store objects, go to Security and you'll see your account there (or the group you belong to, if you get your rights from a group). Scroll through the list of actions ticked... they'll all have to do with list/read/view and none of them will have edit/add/create/write enabled.\n\nNo offense... don't take this the wrong way... but if you don't know what rights are included with the default access roles, you may want to study up a bit before accepting any higher level of access. Exchange can be a beast when it wants to be and an untrained Administrator can cause some serious damage without even knowing it.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: How does dividend imputation work in Australia?
Question: Is it the same for both local and foreign shareholders?
Answer: An arrangement in Australia that eliminates the double taxation of dividends.
 
Double taxation of dividends occurs when both a company and a shareholder pay tax on the same income. The company pays taxes on profits and subsequently distributes a dividend out of their after-tax profits. Shareholders must then pay tax on the dividend received.

The tax imputations indicate to the government that the company issuing the dividend has already paid a portion of the tax due. The shareholder is able to reduce the tax paid on the dividend by the amount of the tax imputation credits.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: Does anyone differentiate between the Investment in Subsidiary and Investment in Associate?
Answer: There are three ways to account for investments - four if you're being really picky. 

1.  A "subsidiary" is where it's parent company owns 50% or more (and has management control).  A subsidiary is consolidated into the parent company's financial statements.  

2.  An "associate" is 20-50% owned, with the parent company not having management control. An associate is reported using equity accounting.

3.  "Cost" accounting is typically used on investments where the parent owns less than 20%.  The investment is reported at the lower of cost or market.  

4. If an investment is held for sale or as inventory, the investment is reported at market.

"Pets become infested with a different kind of scabies mite. If your pet is infested with scabies, (also called mange) and they have close contact with you, the mite can get under your skin and cause itching and skin irritation. However, the mite dies in a couple of days and does not reproduce. The mites may cause you to itch for several days, but you do not need to be treated with special medication to kill the mites. Until your pet is successfully treated, mites can continue to burrow into your skin and cause you to have symptoms."

So the answer to your question is yes they can have scabies, but a different type.

Title: Why would Kahlua (the liqueur) run a sweepstakes only for homeowners? Isn't it poor advertising, and elitist?
Question: Their $20,000 Home Makeover Sweepstakes has no cash option, and the winner must own a home! About 5 states don't/won't allow this, and I suspect it's because it's unfairly limiting. Why would this company limit this form of advertising when homeownership is hardly a requisite for drinking Kahlua? Do they only care if well-off people are customers?

Although they do run numerous contest and givaways for various reasons, something like this is to reach a target market. It is a quick and effective way to have homeowners willing give up their information for later use in a marketing campaign. This is also done with many other products that range from baby items, to beer sponsored events towards college age people. 

It is all about the data and finding a way to to increase the number of drinkers/people buying your product.

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: What's the best way to earn the most interest?
Question: I've finally started saving a lot of money, and I need to know what is the best way to earn a good rate of return, with minimal risk (I'm willing to accept some), no investment cost(load mutual funds, broker fees, etc) and a reasonable level of liquidity (can be accessed after 1 year of deposit without penalty)? This may be asking a lot, but what is your financial strategy for a good steady level of growth?
Answer: That other answer is not entirely true.  CDs are not backed by the full afaith and credit of the US, they are backed by the FDIC.  That means if the bank fails, your deposit is safe.  Only US Treasuries are backed by the full faith and credit of the US government.

You need to figure out how long you plan on saving for.  If it really is one year, then stick with CDs.  If it's 10 years, you should look at high quality mutual funds that have a decent ten year track record (or longer) and are low cost.  

You may want to consider working with an advisor and put together a solid strategy.  Ask your friends and family who they use and then make sure you know how they get paid.  A good advisor is worth the price, just make sure you find one that's ethical!

label: Computers & Internet
sentence:
Title: how to disable the Back and Refresh buttons of the browser in an ASP web application?
Question: I basically want the users not to use browser's back button by either disabling it or by giving a message
Answer: This was asked in an earlier question.  But the basic answer is, you can't (and you definitely can't do it with a server side script like ASP).  Even if you could, you still have other ways that the user can navigate back into the middle of a session.  There are the forward/back options in the context menu (right click on a page), there is the browswer history, the user can simply enter an explicit url.\n\nSo bottom line is you can't do it and even if you could you need to make your web app robust enough to handle users plopping themselves in the middle of defunct sessions and/or repeating actions.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: What happens to the level of the water when you do this?
Question: You load your recently murdered latest victim, wrapped in a large sack which also contains heavy rocks, into your boat and row to the middle of a 50 acre lake. What happens to the water level of the lake when you throw the body overboard?
Answer: The water level will go down when you throw the body in.  Try it in your sink.  Fill your sink with water and float a bowl (boat) in it.  Then, fill the bowl with silver wear (body and rocks) or anything more dense than water.  Mark where the water level is on the side of the sink.  Now, take the silver wear out, and put it in the water so it falls to the bottom of the sink.  You will notice that the water level went down.  When the silver was in the bowl, it displaced much more water than when it was at the bottom of the sink.  Therefore, the body will displace more water when it is inside the boat than when it falls into the water.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: What is seroprevalence?
Answer: Seroprevalence is the number of persons in a population who test positive for a specific disease based on serology (blood serum) specimens; often presented as a percent of the total specimens tested or as a rate per 100,000 persons tested. As positively identifying the occurrence of disease is usually based upon the presence of antibodies for that disease (especially with viral infections such as Herpes Simplex and HIV), this number is not significant if the specificity of the antibody is low.

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: Approximately how much do high school English teachers earn per annum in the USA?
Answer: Teachers are paid on a salary schedule, which is based on highest college degree earned, and years of experience.  (Of course, add on fringe benefits like insurance, and your retirement plan, which will let you retire nicely with 25 or 30 years, or at age 62 or 65.)  \n\nTeacher salary can vary greatly, depending on location, degree earned, and years of experience.  School districts that pay the most often have the highest costs of living (housing cost, etc.)  In Florida, beginning teacher salary (bachelor's degree, no experience) is $27,000 - 30,000.  Some districts pay more; others less.  Fortunately now, salary schedules are usually available at school district web-sites.  Decide where you want to live, and do a Yahoo search for the local school district web-site.  You will probably be able to find a salary schedule online.  If not, just call the school district personnel office, and they will be happy to tell you.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Question: why will old gram-positive cells stain  gram-negative?
Answer: Gram positive cells stain gram positive because of teichoic acid that binds the peptidoglycan layers of the cell walls together. Gram positive cells have 25 to 30 layers of peptidoglycan in their walls, whereas gram negative cells only have one or two layers. As the cells age  their walls start to break down and cannot retain the stain. They lose layers of their peptioglycan or the teichoic acid isn't as effective. I hope that helps.

Title: How do you know when it time to move on in your job? Or do I need to adjust my attitude?
Question: I do love my job, but I feel like I am being wasted because they like what I am doing in my present situation. I do feel however that I have so much more to give and they are not giving me a chance to grow and see that I can do much more then they are allowing me to. I am a receptionist and have been for 6 years with the same company and we have had many recent changes and I used to do more for them, and now I am not challenged and bored out of my mind!
Answer: Don't settle for a job that gets stagnant. I worked for 12 years as a service rep for a huge telephone company, had opportunities to advance to a supervisors position, (didn't want the headaches) but the politics were overwhelming. I resigned over a year ago. I feel much better physically and mentally! I have a small business to fall back on, which is doing pretty well, to suppliment my husbands income. \n\nIf you stay with a job that gets to you like that, eventually, you will start having physical problems. Mine got so bad that I ended up going into Congestive Heart Failure 2 years ago. To make a long story short, since I left, I've gotten a clean bill of health (which is most important!), and I have a lot more patience with people in general.

Title: Is there anyway to get bigger boobs?!?!?
Question: Are there exercises you could do.
I drink milk everyday.
Its just that nothing is working and i'm 16 years old!
Answer: Hormone treatment and perhaps surgury (to be avoided)

Aside from surgery and altering your hormone balance (e.g. birth control pills), neither of which are recommended, I don't know of any way to enlarge breasts. 

People say there are exercises that in the past were recommended for breast enlargement. Basically, you lift your arms and thrust your chest in and out repeatedly. These  exercises will not exactly change the size of your breasts. Breasts are largely fatty tissue, and there are no exercises to grow fat. However, the exercise  helps to build up the chest muscles directly below the breasts. This can push the breasts outward. Even though not enlarged, the fact that they will protrude more gives them the appearance of being a bit large.

Given the facts my advice is if you're just looking for a more appearance-altering technique, I recommend a well-padded bra.

Best wishes

label: Education & Reference
sentence:
Question: How does getting married affect financial aid for a student?
Answer: Being married is one of the criteria for being considered independent for Federal Student Aid purposes.  Being considered independent, in turn, means that

(a) you are no longer required to include your parents' income/asset information on your FAFSA
(b) you must include your spouse's income/asset information on your FAFSA

This can be a good or bad thing, depending on how much money your spouse makes.  Most undergraduate students would benefit from this status simply because their spouse would probably make less than their parents did.  However, it is worth noting that there are instances in which a student would become *less* needy by marrying (and therfore, potentially eligible for less financial aid)...

(1) If you are a graduate student, you would already be considered independent and therefore would *already* not be including your parents' information on your FAFSA.  Were you to marry someone with any kind of income or assets, you would have to report those amounts, so it is likely that your EFC would increase with this newfound "wealth."

(2) Independent students are subject to a different EFC formula than dependent students.  The calculation is far too long to display here, but if you're curious as to what it takes into consideration, check here: http://www.ifap.ed.gov/efcinformation/attachments/0607EFCFormulaGuideDecFinal.pdf

Most notable of everything in this link is that the income and asset "conversion rates" are higher for independent students than they are for dependent students.  What does this mean?  Say, for example, that your new spouse had exactly the same income/assets as your parents.  Even if everything else was the same (age, number in household, state of residence), your EFC as the married student would be higher because your spouse's income/assets would be "converted" (i.e. counted against you) at a higher rate than if they belonged to your parents.
 
As far as actual awards...

If is worth noting, that if you are an independent *undergraduate* student, you will be entitled to an additional $4,000 in Unsubsidized Stafford Loan funding (this number increases to $5,000 for your Junior and Senior years).  Note: Graduate students are eligible for much more Stafford loan funding than undergrads.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: wheel A of radius rA = 10 cm is coupled by belt B to wheel C of?
Question: wheel A of radius rA = 10 cm is coupled by belt B to wheel C of radius rC = 25 cm. The angular speed of wheel A is increased from rest at a constant rate of 1.6 rad/sec2. Find the time needed for wheel C to reach an angular speed of 100 rev/min, assuming the belt does not slip. (Hint: If the belt does not slip, the linear speeds at the two rims must be equal.)
Answer: let RA and RC be the angular speed of wheel A and C respectively
then linear speed of belt B = RA*rA = RC*rC 
hence RA= RC * rC/rA = RC 25/10 = 2.5 RC

When C reach 100 rev/min
then A reach 2.5 100 = 250 rev/min
RA = RAo + a*t = 0 + 1.6 t rad / sec
RA = 1.6 t /(2*pi) = 1.6 t /6.28 =0.2548 t rev/sec =15.288 t rev/min
15.288 t = 250
t = 250/15.288 = 16.35  seconds

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Title: What's the best way to include reference from my boss I now work with?
Question: I work for a temp agency and my assignment is ending (one year assignment).  I want to include a reference from my supervisors at my work to my resume/application.  Would I just have them as a reference, or could I have them write a letter of recomendation?  Should I go with two or stick with only one?  how long should the recomendation be?  Personal experience only please.
Answer: Ask for a written letter of recommendation.  People move on, you might not be able to track him down in the future.  Present him with a written reminder list of things you have done while on that one year assignment.  

I have 3 letters of recommendation which have helped me tremendously. 2 of the 3 bosses are no longer with the company I worked for.  I include them when I apply for some jobs because I have moved to another state.  So instead of asking future employers to make a long distance phone call, they have that letter right there.  They probably still want to call if I am a serious candidiate for the job, but in the meantime they have proof of satisfying some other former employers.  

When you work for a temp agency, so many times you are just "that temp guy".  If you have established a rapport with someone at an assignment, I think it's vital for you to get a letter.  The HR department at that company can't verify what you did, they can only see that for XX amount of time, they had someone outsourced from XX company.  You didn't technically work for that firm, you are employed techincally by the temp agency.  That is why it's even more important for somone in your situation to ask for letters if people are willing to write them.

Good luck on your next gig!

label: Business & Finance
sentence:
Question: what do you need to reinstate a suspended driver's license in VA?
Answer: From the DMV's website:

If your license is suspended, your privilege to drive has been withdrawn temporarily. You may pay the required fees and reinstate your license at the end of the suspension period unless your license expires during that period. You must also show proof of legal presence.
Revocation, on the other hand, means that your privilege to drive has been terminated. Your driving privilege may be restored if you reapply for a driver’s license after the revocation period has passed. You must show proof of legal presence. You must successfully complete the vision screening, two-part knowledge exam and road skills tests and pay the required fees when you reapply.

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: Help! I need help with advanced algebra!?
Question: Please help me answer this question:
The volume of gas varies directly as its temperature measured in degrees Kelvin and inversly as its pressure. When the temperature of a certain gas is 250 degrees Kelvin and its pressure is 76.2 cm of mercury, its volume is 750 square cm. 
a) Find the constant of variation
b) Write a variable equation that models the situation using the constant of variation found in part a
c) What would the volume of this gas be to the nearest cubic centimeter if its temperature were 300 degrees Kelvin and its presure were 73.06 cm of mercury?
Answer: V = kT/P, where V = volume, T = temp, P = pressure and k is the constant of proportionality.
You are given, T = 250, P = 76.2 and V = 750.
Using this information, you can solve for k.
a) 750 = k(250)/76.2
k = 750(76.2)/250
k = 228.6
b) Go back to the equation at the top and replace k with 228.6
V = 228.6T/P
c) You are given T = 300 and P = 73.06
Plug these in to find V.
V = 228.6(300)/73.06
V = 938.68

label: Sports
sentence:
Title: how can i get better at the 400 dash?
Question: how can i prepare for the 400 meter dash? i want to get 60 secs.?
i want to run it in 60 seconds flat. any techniques? work outs?

PLEASE DON'T SAY KEEP RUNNING. I NEED EXCERCISES, ENDURANCE, QUOTES. etc.
Answer: Here is some advice I previously posted to another 400m runner.

If you aren't already running 600m in your workout, add them once a week. They build up your strength and help you maintain your speed over that last 100m.  Not knowing your ability, I would say try to run them going out the first 400 at a pace 5-10 seconds slower than your best 400m and then concentrate on staying strong to the finish.

I would also to workouts with multiple 400s, 5-10 seconds slower than your best 400 time, or multiple 200s, a second or two faster than your 400m pace.

You might also try running shorter intevals up a long hill. Back in the 60's, the UCLA team used to run 400s up a long hill. They had the best 4x400 team in the country back then, so the there is evidence that the workouts did some good. 

You need to learn how to run the race correctly. I break the race down into 4 100s. For most of the first 100, you should be accelerating smoothly to your "cruising pace", running hard, but not sprinting all out. The 2nd and 3rd 100s, should be fast, but relaxed. The last 100 is where your strength will have to kick in. You are going to start to feel like someone dropped a weight on your back. We used to say "The bear got him" when we'd watch it happen to a runner in a big way.
 
You'll need to focus on keeping you knees going straight forward. They will want to go off at an angle.  Keep your arms swinging straight ahead. Some runners feel it helps them to exagerate their armswing as it helps keep their legs going.  

Relax your face and jaw. Don't grimace. When you do, it thightens up first your face, then your neck and shoulders.

After a hard run, do a lot of stretching. You've got to have a full range of motion to have a smooth, effortless stride.

I always recommend Bob Anderson's stretching book, it's a classic: http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0936070226/002-4340010-1751209\

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: Serious Calc help please...I'm lost and I have a huge test tomorrow that I am studying for?
Question: Problem: A tin can is made with thicker tin on top and bottom than on the side.  The tin used for the top and bottom costs 20% more than the material for the side.  FInd the dimensions of the can that minimize cost if the can must have a volume of 5 cu. in

Basically I have a whole section of optimization problems like this...mostly these are going to be on my test.  Not only can you show me how to solve it, but can you also please help explain what different steps you take in order to solve either finding maxima or minima.  Thank you.
Answer: V = πr²h
A (top & bottom) = πr²
End Area = 2πr²
Lateral Area = 2πrh
Cost of tube = 2πrhC, C being cost per unit area
Cost of ends = 2πr²C*1.2
Total cost, P = 2πC(1.2r² + rh)
h = V/πr², so
P = 2πC(1.2r² + rV/πr²)
P = 2πC(1.2r² + V/πr)
dP/dr = 2πC(2.4r - V/πr²) = 0 for max or min, so
2.4r = V/πr²
r³ = V/2.4π
r³ = 5/2.4π
r = 0.872"
h = 5/π0.872²
h = 2.093"

Check:
C(2.4*π0.872² + 10/0.872) = 17.201050C
C(2.4*π0.87² + 10/0.87) = 17.201444C (+0.000394)
C(2.4*π0.874² + 10/0.874) = 17.201137C (+0.000087)

label: Entertainment & Music
sentence:
Question: Is allofmp3.com and mp3sugar.com really legal?
Answer: Google "is allofmp3 legal" and you will find this nice answer from the EFF :
"Thanks for writing and sorry for the late reply. Before I say anything, I should point out that I am not an attorney and this is not legal advice. Unfortunately, the situation with AllofMP3 is, like many things, too good to be true. Whatever licenses or loopholes the site claims to have in Russia (which I would take with multiple grains of salt... or shots of vodka?), they don't extend to a U.S. citizen who downloads the content inside the U.S. That's where the copying takes place, and that's where the parties have to have a license to make copies. If they don't (and they don't), then you could be in trouble if the files were found on your computer. One interesting extra-legal thing to consider is that it's harder for the RIAA to get the names of people who use AllofMP3 in order to prosecute them, but that may not be true forever. Hope that helps!"

PS
To me, it does not seem very difficult to get the names of those that payed with a credit card...

Title: An 800kg car coasts down a hill 40m high with its engine off and the driver's foot pressing into the brake ...

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: Rectangle rotation calculation...?
Question: Say I have a rectangle center at 0,0. width = 10 and height = 20. So the four corners are (-5, 10)....etc. After I rotate it say 30 degrees. What are the four new corner values? What is the general formula to calculate if there is one.
Answer: The general rotation transform function is to multiply its coordinate in complex coordination by the transform function of R(teta)=cos(teta)+i*sin(teta) where teta is the transform (rotation) angle and i is the imaginary unique value.
Suppose coordination of your point is (x,y); so the transformed point (x',y') would be:
x'+i*y'=(x+i*y)*
        [cos(teta)+i*sin(teta)]
       =[x*cos(teta)-y*sin(teta)]+
        i*[x*sin(teta)+y*cos(teta)]
therefore:
x'=x*cos(teta)-y*sin(teta)
y'=x*sin(teta)+y*cos(teta)

in your example, the point (-5,10) after rotation of 30 degrees counterclockwise will be transformed to:
x'=-5*cos(30)-10*sin(30)~ -9.33
y'=-5*sin(30)+10*cos(30)~ +6.16

label: Politics & Government
sentence:
Title: Does anyone know who do you report the police to when they break the law and fabricate lies that hurt people?
Question: PLEASE DONT TELL ME THE POLICE COMPLAINT CENTER BECAUSE I HAVE YET TO SEE ANY RESULTS FROM THEM, THEY ONLY TOOK $200.00 FROM ME, AND NEVER RETURNED MY PHONECALLS,FAXES OR E-MAIL?
Answer: What type of laws did they break?  Unless they’ve broken a criminal law, internal affairs can’t do anything.  If you’re talking about a false arrest or getting caught in a sting operation, the best recourse is to appeal the conviction.   Almost 100% of the time the lower courts will side with the police officer, but in the appeals court, the decision often swings toward the plaintiff.

Fabricating lies and spreading them with the intention of causing harm to another person is a civil matter.  Slander and libel are difficult to prove.  First you must prove the person is lying, then you must prove they are spreading those lies with the sole intention of hurting you and then you must prove those lies have caused you to lose something i.e.: a job, money, your home, etc.  Call an attorney and see if you have a case, but chances are, you can’t do anything.

If you have a video tape which contradicts even one sentence of the police report, you should appeal the case in a higher court.  All you have to do is catch them in one little lie to have the whole thing thrown out of court.

label: Sports
sentence:
Question: What's the shortest possible explanation of the offside rule?
Answer: If, when the ball is last touched by a teammate, is the player in question closer to the opponent's goal line than:
1) the half line?
2) the ball?
3) the second to the last opponent?

If you answer yes to all of these, then he was in an offside position when the ball was touched and you can ask the last question:
4) Did he gain an advantage from his position?

If you say yes to all questions, the player is guilty of an offside infraction.

However, you cannot be found guilty of an offside infraction if you receive the ball directly from a: goal-kick, throw-in, or corner kick.

label: Family & Relationships
sentence:
Title: When is it okay to tell everyone you're gay?
Question: I mean i know that it's probably a comfort thing and a time thing but is there an actual "right" time?
Answer: Honestly, I don't think there's a "right" time to tell everyone.  Some people you may feel comfortable telling much sooner than others.  Some people you may not feel it's a good idea to tell at all.

I would say the point that's the "right" time is one of two times:

1)  When you know that the person you're telling will accept and care for you because of who you are and that means they'll accept you for being gay, too... and you want to be open with them about who you are inside; or

2)  When it hurts you so much to hide yourself that being rejected for who you are is less painful about hiding who you are from people.

Those are generally the times you're going to come out to someone... 

In any case, I hope if you're asking this for personal reasons, it works out and you don't get hurt by anyone for just being the person you are.  =)

label: Science & Mathematics
sentence:
Title: How do I begin to solve for n if Pn,4 = 56Pn,2 ?
Question: I can get this far n!/(n-4)! = 56 [n!/(n-2)!] 

then I am stuck.

thank you
Answer: so far, so good

n!/(n-4)! = 56 [n!/(n-2)!] 

multiply both sides by (n-2)!/n!

[n!(n-2)!] / [n!(n-4)!] = 56

(n-2)! / (n-4)! = 56

(n-2)(n-3) = 56

You could multiply this out and solve the quadratic, in which case you will get one positive and one negative root, or if you remember that 56 = 8*7, then that quickly leads to n = 10
